ahile kati bajyo — pāṭanako antima basa chuṭlā jasto cha
Come tomorrow before nine; we will start cooking early.
भोलि नौ बजेभन्दा अघि आउनुहोस्; छिटो खाना पकाउँछौं।
bholi nau bajebhandā aghi āunuhos; chiṭo khānā pakāu~chauṃ
I will come later after I help my mother clean the kitchen.
आमालाई भान्सा सफा गर्न मद्दत गरेर म पछि आउनेछु।
āmālāī bhānsā safā garna maddata garera ma pachi āune-chu

title: Verbs indicating cause of action (Causative verb) in Nepali
content: Causative: to make someone do. पढ़ (read) → पढ़ाब (make read/teach).
intro: First causative often -आ-; second -वा-. कर → कराब (get done), खा → खुवाब (feed).

blocks.heading: Causative verb examples
rows: I make him eat vegetables every day because he is still growing.
उ बढिरहेकाले म उसलाई हरेक दिन तरकारी खान लगाउँछु।
u baḍhirihe-kāle ma usalāī hareka dina tarkārī khāna lagāu~chu
She teaches small children to read letters in the village school.
उनी गाउँको विद्यालयमा साना बच्चालाई अक्षर पढाउँछिन्।
unī gāu~ko vidyālayamā sānā baccālāī akṣara paḍhāu~chin
Get this form signed today; the office will close early on Friday.
आजै फाराममा दस्तखत गर्नुहोस्; शुक्रबार कार्यालय छिटो बन्द हुन्छ।
ājai phārāmamā dastakhata garnuhos; śukrabāra kāryālaya chiṭo banda huncha
I had him write the address clearly on both sides of the envelope.
मैले उसलाई खाम दुवैतिर ठेगाना स्पष्ट लेख्न लगाएँ।
maile usalāī khāma duvaitira ṭhegāna spaṣṭa lekhna lagāe~
Feed the child warm rice; the doctor said he needs more energy.
बच्चालाई न्यानो भात खुवाउनुहोस्; डाक्टरले ऊर्जा चाहिन्छ भने।
baccālāī nyāno bhāta khuvāunuhos; ḍākṭarale ūrjā cāhincha bhane
He got the roof repaired before the monsoon rain started.
मनसुनको पानी सुरु नहुँदै उसले छाना मर्मत गरायो।
manasunako pānī suru nahu~dai usale chānā marmata garāyo
Show me the ticket; I need to check the seat number for you.
टिकट देखाउनुहोस्; तपाईंको सिट नम्बर जाँच गर्नु छ।
ṭikaṭa dekhāunuhos; tapāīṃko siṭa nambara jā̃ca garnu cha
Let me know when you arrive so I can meet you at the gate.
I will make you understand the rule step by step with examples.
She sent him to the shop with a list and enough money.
उसलाई सूची र पैसा दिएर पसल पठाइन्।
usalāī sūcī ra paisā dieera pasala paṭhāin
Heavy rain can cause rocks to fall from the steep cliff.
भारी पानीले उच्च चट्टानबाट ढुङ्गा झर्न सक्छ।
bhārī pānīle ucca caṭṭānanabāṭa ḍhu~gā jharna sak-cha
Bright sunlight can cause this room to feel very hot at noon.
दिउँसो घामले यो कोठा धेरै तातो बनाउँछ।
diuṃso ghāmale yo koṭhā dherai tāto banāu~cha
Offer juice to the guests first; later we will serve the main meal.
पहिले पाहुनालाई जुस दिनुहोस्; पछि मुख्य खाना।
pahile pāhunālāī jusa dinuhos; pachi mukhya khānā
They had us wait in the hall while they prepared the documents.
कागजात तयार गर्दा उनीहरूले हामीलाई हलमा पर्खाइन्।
kāgjāt tayāra gardā unīharūle hāmīlāī halamā parkhāin
Do not make him cry; he is already nervous about the exam.
उसलाई नरुवाउनुहोस्; परीक्षामा उ अझै घबराएको छ।
usalāī naruvāunuhos; parīkṣāmā u ajhai ghabarāeko cha

title: Gender-number agreement rule in Nepali
content: In Nepali, grammatical gender/number agreement is limited. Adjectives may agree optionally.
intro: Optional agreement in adjectives (सुन्दर/सुन्दरी). No verb agreement with gender.

blocks.heading: Agreement examples
rows: The handsome actor looked confident on stage under the bright lights.
उज्यालो बत्तीमुनि मञ्चमा सुन्दर अभिनेता आत्मविश्वासी देखिए।
ujyālo battīmunimañcamā sundara abhinētā ātmaviśvāsī dekhie
The talented actress sang softly while the rain tapped on the roof.
छानामा पानी पर्दा प्रतिभाशाली अभिनेत्री मधुर गाइन्।
chānāmā pānī pardā pratibhāśālī abhinetrī madhura gāin
We live in a big house with a garden where my grandmother grows herbs.
हामी ठूलो घरमा बस्छौं जहाँ हजुरआमाले जडीबुटी लगाउनुहुन्छ।
hāmī ṭhūlo gharamā bas-chauṃ jahā̃ hajurāmāle jaḍībuṭī lagāunuhuncha
That big tree gives shade to travellers who rest on the village path.
त्यो ठूलो रूख गाउँको बाटोमा आराम गर्ने यात्रीलाई छहारी दिन्छ।
yo ṭhūlo rūkha gāu~ko bāṭomā ārāma garne yātrīlāī chahārī din-cha
My grammar book is on the desk next to your blue notebook.
तिम्रो नीलो कापी नजिकको टेबलमा मेरो व्याकरणको किताब छ।
timro nīlo kāpī najikako ṭebalamā mero vyākaraṇako kitāba cha
Our house faces east, so the kitchen is bright every morning.
हाम्रो घर पूर्व मुख भएकाले बिहान भान्सा उज्यालो हुन्छ।
hāmro ghara pūrva mukha bhaekāle bihāna bhānsā ujyālo huncha
The polite boy opened the door for the elderly woman.
विनम्र केटाले बुढी आमाको लागि ढोका खोलिदिए।
vinamra keṭāle buḍhī āmākā lāgi ḍhokā kholidie
The clever girl solved the puzzle before anyone else in the class.
कक्षामा अरूभन्दा पहिले चलाख केटीले पहेली सफा गरिन्।
kakṣāmā arūbhanda pahile calākh keṭīle pahelī safā garin
A white horse galloped across the field while the crowd cheered.
भीड कराउँदा सेतो घोडा मैदानमा दौडियो।
bhīḍ karāu~dā seto ghoḍā maidānamā dauḍiyo
The black dog wags its tail when it sees children with biscuits.
बिस्कुट बोकेर बच्चा देख्दा कालो कुकुर पुच्छर हल्लाउँछ।
biskuṭa bokera bacca dekh-dā kālo kukura pucchara hallāu~cha
She is beautiful inside and out, and everyone respects her.
उनी भित्रबाहिर सुन्दर छिन्, सबैले सम्मान गर्छन्।
unī bhitra-bāhira sundara chin, sabaile sammāna gar-chan
He is tall enough to reach the top shelf without a stool.
उहाँ अग्लो हुनुहुन्छ; स्टुल बिना माथिलो ताक पुग्नुहुन्छ।
uhā~ aglo hunuhuncha; sṭula binā māthilo tāka pugnuhuncha
That house is big, but it needs paint and a new roof soon.
त्यो घर ठूलो छ, तर चाँडै रङ र नयाँ छानो चाहिन्छ।
tyo ghara ṭhūlo cha, tara cā̃ḍai raṅa ra nayā~ chāno cāhincha
I bought a new book about Nepali folk tales at the fair.
मेलामा नेपाली लोक कथाको नयाँ किताब किनेँ।
melāmā nepālī loka kathāko nayā~ kitāba kine~
The old man sits in the sun and tells stories to the village children.
बुढा मान्छे घाममा बसेर गाउँका बच्चालाई कथा सुनाउँछन्।
buḍhā mānche ghāmamā basera gāu~kā baccālāī kathā sunāu~chan

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Tense – Second Style in Nepali
content: Alternative perfect: using लेल (taken) or गेल (gone) as auxiliary with past participle.
intro: Have done = केल + लेल छी. Had done = केल + लेल छल.

blocks.heading: Perfect second style
rows: I have eaten nothing since dawn, so I feel a little weak now.
बिहानदेखि केही नखाएकाले अहिले अलि कमजोर लाग्छ।
bihānadekhi kehī nakhāekāle ahile ali kamajora lāgcha
You have come at the right moment; we were just about to start.
ठ्याक्कै समयमा आउनुभयो; सुरु गर्न लाग्दै थियौं।
ṭhyākkai samayamā āunubhayo; suru garna lāgdai thiyauṃ
He has gone to the bank, but he will return before lunch.
उहाँ बैंक जानुभएको छ; दिउँसो खानाअघि फर्कनुहुन्छ।
uhā~ baiṅk jānubhaeko cha; diuṃso khānāaghi pharkanuhuncha
Have you finished copying the notes, or do you need five more minutes?
नोट कापी सक्नुभयो, कि अझै पाँच मिनेट चाहिन्छ?
noṭa kāpī saknubhayo, ki ajhai pāñca mineṭa cāhincha
I had eaten at home, so I only ordered tea at the restaurant.
घरमा खाइसकेकाले रेस्टुरेन्टमा चिया मात्र मगाएँ।
gharamā khāisakekāle resṭurenṭamā ciyā mātra magāe~
She had left before we arrived, so we missed her at the station.
हामी आइपुग्नुअघि नै उनी गइसकेकी थिइन्।
hāmī āipugnuaghi nai unī gaisakekī thiin
We will have done the work by Friday if nothing unexpected happens.
अनपेक्षित केही नभए शुक्रबारसम्म काम सकिनेछ।
anapekṣita kehī nabhae śukrabārasamma kāma sakine-cha
They will have arrived by night if the bus does not break down.
बस बिग्रेन भने रातिसम्म आइपुगेका हुनेछन्।
bas bigrena bhane rātisamma āipugekā hune-chan
I have understood the main points after reading the chapter twice.
अध्याय दुई पटक पढेर मुख्य बुँदा बुझेको छु।
adhyāya duī paṭaka paḍhera mukhya bu~dā bujheko chu
You have seen this film before, but the ending still surprises me.
यो फिल्म अघि नै देख्नु भएको छ, तर अन्त्य अझै अचम्म लाग्छ।
yo philm aghi nai dekhnubhaeko cha, tara antya ajhai acamma lāgcha
After having eaten, we walked slowly along the river bank.
Having come so far, we cannot turn back without reaching the top.
यति टाढा आएपछि शिखर नपुगी फर्कन मिल्दैन।
yati ṭāḍā āepachi śikhara napugī pharkana mildaina
I am finished with cleaning; now you can bring the boxes inside.
सफाइ सकिएको छ; अब बाकस भित्र ल्याउनुहोस्।
safāi sakieko cha; aba bākasa bhitra lyāunuhos
She has written three pages about her village for the school magazine.
विद्यालय पत्रिकाका लागि उनले गाउँबारे तीन पृष्ठ लेखेकी छिन्।
vidyālaya patrikākā lāgi unale gāu~bāre tīna pr̥ṣṭha lekhekī chin
They have played in every district tournament and won many cups.
उनीहरुले जिल्लास्तरका हरेक प्रतियोगितामा खेलेका छन् र धेरै कप जितेका छन्।
unīharule jillāstarakā hareka pratiyogitāmā khelekā chan ra dherai kapa jitekā chan

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Continuous tense in Nepali
content: Perfect continuous: have been doing, had been doing. Past participle + रहल + छी/छल.
intro: Have been eating = खाबैत रहल + छी. Had been going = जाइत रहल + छल.

blocks.heading: Perfect continuous
rows: I have been eating small meals every two hours on the doctor’s advice.
डाक्टरको सल्लाहमा दुई घन्टामा अलिअलि खाइरहेको छु।
ḍākṭarako sallāhamā duī ghanṭāmā ali-ali khāiriheko chu
You have been going to the gym regularly, and it really shows.
नियमित जिम जाँदै हुनुहुन्छ, फरक देखिन्छ।
niyamita jima jā~dai hunuhuncha, pharka dekhincha
He has been reading the same novel for weeks because it is very long.
हप्तौंदेखि उही लामो उपन्यास पढिरहेका छन्।
haptau~dekhi uhī lāmo upanyāsa paḍhirahekā chan
She has been working night shifts, so she looks tired this morning.
रातको पालो काम गरिरहेकी छिन्, बिहान थाकेकी देखिन्छिन्।
rātako pālo kāma garirahekī chin, bihāna thākekī dekhin-chin
We have been waiting here since eight; the bus is very late.
आठ बजेदेखि यहीँ पर्खिरहेका छौं; बस धेरै ढिलो।
āṭha bajedekhi yahī~ parkhirahēkā chauṃ; basa dherai ḍhilo
They have been playing music in the street to collect money for charity.
दानका लागि उनीहरूले गल्लीमा संगीत बजाइरहेका छन्।
dānakā lāgi unīharūle gallīmā saṃgīta bajāirahekā chan
I had been studying all night, so I slept through the alarm.
रातभरि पढ्दा अलार्म बज्दा पनि सुतिरहेँ।
rātabhari paḍhdā alārma bajdā pani sutirahe~
You had been sleeping when I called, so I did not wake you.
फोन गर्दा तिमी सुतिरहेका थियौ, त्यसैले नउठाएँ।
phona gardā timī sutirahekā thiyau, tyasaile na uṭhāe~
He had been running before the race, so his muscles were warm.
दौडअघि तयारी गर्दा मांसपेशी न्यानो थियो।
dauḍaghi tayārī gardā māṃsameśī nyāno thiyo
Since morning I have been waiting for the courier with my passport.
बिहानैदेखि राहदानी ल्याउने कुरियर पर्खिरहेको छु।
bihānaidekhi rāhadānī lyāune kuriyar parkhirheko chu
For two hours she has been cooking for twenty guests in our kitchen.
दुई घन्टादेखि उनी हाम्रो भान्सामा बीस जनाको लागि खाना पकाउँदै छिन्।
duī ghanṭādekhi unī hāmro bhānsāmā bīsa janākā lāgi khānā pakāu~dai chin
I have been thinking about your idea, and I agree with most of it.
तपाईंको विचारबारे सोच्दै आएको छु; धेरै सहमत छु।
tapāīṃko vicārabāre socdai āeko cha; dherai sahamata chu
They had been talking for hours before they finally reached a decision.
निर्णय हुन घन्टौं कुरा गरिरहेका थिए।
nirṇaya huna ghanṭauṃ kurā garirahekā thie
We will have been living in this city for ten years next spring.
अर्को वसन्तमा यस सहरमा दस वर्ष बाँचिरहेका हुनेछौं।
arko vasanta yasa saharama dasa varṣa bā~cirahekā hune-chauṃ
It really takes time to learn a new language if you study alone.
एक्लै सिक्दा नयाँ भाषा सिक्न साँच्चै समय लाग्छ।
eklai sikdā nayā~ bhāṣā sikna sā~ccai samaya lāgcha

title: Difference between "no/not" and "don't want" in Nepali
content: नहि/नै = no, not. "Don't want" = नहि चाही, चाहब नहि, or नको (like Hindi नको).
intro: Negation vs refusal. I don't want = हमरा नहि चाही. No thanks = नै, धन्यवाद.

blocks.heading: No/not vs don't want
rows: No, there is no sugar left in the jar on the kitchen shelf.
छैन, भान्साको ताकमा भएको बट्टामा चिनी बाँकी छैन।
chaina, bhānsāko tākama bhaeko baṭṭāmā cinī bā~kī chaina
That is not my umbrella; mine has a blue stripe on the handle.
त्यो मेरो छाता होइन; मेरोमा ह्यान्डलमा नीलो धर्को छ।
tyo mero chātā hoina; meromā hyāṇḍalamā nīlo dharko cha
I do not want any more rice; my stomach is already full.
I do not want this old phone; I am saving for a better one.
यो पुरानो फोन चाहिँदैन; राम्रो किन्न जोगाइरहेको छु।
yo purāno phona cāhi~daina; rāmro kinna jogāiriheko chu
No, thank you — I have already eaten dinner at my aunt’s house.
होइन, धन्यवाद — काकीको घरमा खाना खाइसकेको छु।
hoina, dhanyavāda — kākīko gharamā khānā khāisakeko chu
I do not need another blanket; this one is warm enough for me.
अर्को कम्बल चाहिँदैन; यो न्यानो छ।
arko kamabala cāhi~daina; yo nyāno cha
I do not want to go out tonight; the roads are slippery in the rain.
आज बाहिर जान मन लाग्दैन; पानीले बाटो चिप्लो छ।
āja bāhira jāna mana lāgdaina; pānīle bāṭo ciplo cha
He does not want to sell the land; he hopes to build a house there.
उ जग्गा बेच्न चाहँदैन; घर बनाउन आशा छ।
u jaggā bechna cāha~daina; ghara banāuna āśā cha
We do not want trouble with the neighbours over a small matter.
I am not interested in that job because the salary is too low.
तलब कम भएकाले त्यो जागिरमा चासो छैन।
talaba kama bhaekāle tyo jāgiramā cāso chaina
It is not necessary to print everything; we can read on the screen.
सबै छाप्न आवश्यक छैन; स्क्रिनमा पढ्न सकिन्छ।
sabai chāpna āvaśyaka chaina; skrinamā paḍhna sakinch
No problem — we can change the date if everyone agrees.
कुनै समस्या छैन; सबै माने मिति बदल्न सकिन्छ।
kunai samasyā chaina; sabai māne miti badalna sakinch
I cannot afford this coat right now, but maybe next month.
अहिले यो कोट किन्न सक्दिन; अर्को महिना हुन सक्छ।
ahile yo koṭa kinna sakdina; arko mahinā huna sak-cha
You do not have to wait for me; I might be very late tonight.
मेरो लागि पर्खनु पर्दैन; आज रात धेरै ढिलो हुन सक्छ।
mero lāgi parkhanu pardaina; āja rāta dherai ḍhilo huna sak-cha
I do not want this cold drink; please bring hot tea instead.
यो चिसो पेय चाहिँदैन; तातो चिया ल्याउनुहोस्।
yo ciso peya cāhi~daina; tāto ciyā lyāunuhos

title: Sentence using "Going to" phrase in Nepali
content: "Going to" for future intention. जाब लागल, आबब लागल.
intro: Examples: म जाँदै छु (I am going / about to go), म खान लाग्छु (I am about to eat).

blocks.heading: Going to phrase
rows: I am going to go to the bank before it closes this afternoon.
दिउँसो बैंक बन्द नहुँदै म जाँदै छु।
diuṃso baiṅk banda nahu~dai ma jā~dai chu
You are going to come with us if the rain stops before noon.
पानी बिहानै रोकियो भने तिमी हामीसँग आउन लागेका छौ।
pānī bihānai rokiyo bhane timī hāmīsa~ga āuna lāgekā chau
He is going to eat lunch with his colleagues at the new restaurant.
उहाँ सहकर्मीसँग नयाँ रेस्टुरेन्टमा दिउँसो खाना खान जाँदै हुनुहुन्छ।
uhā~ sahakarmīsa~ga nayā~ resṭurenṭamā diuṃso khānā khāna jā~dai hunuhuncha
She is going to read the report again before the meeting starts.
बैठक सुरु नहुँदै उनी प्रतिवेदन फेरि पढ्न जाँदैछिन्।
baiṭhaka suru nahu~dai unī prativedana pheri paḍhna jā~daichin
We are going to play a friendly match on the school field on Saturday.
शनिबार विद्यालय मैदानमा हामी मैत्रीपूर्ण खेल खेल्न जाँदैछौं।
śanibāra vidyālaya maidānamā hāmī maitrīpūrṇa khela khelna jā~daichauṃ
They are going to leave for the airport as soon as they pack the bags.
झोला प्याक गर्नेबित्तिकै उनीहरू विमानस्थल जान लागेका छन्।
jholā pyāka garnēbittikai unīharū vimānasthala jāna lāgekā chan
I am going to study all evening because the exam is next week.
अर्को हप्ता परीक्षा भएकाले म साँझभरि अध्ययन गर्न जाँदैछु।
arko haptā parīkṣā bhaekāle ma sā~jhabhari adhyayana garna jā~daichu
You are going to write three pages for your homework tonight.
आज रात तपाईं तीन पृष्ठ गृहकार्य लेख्न जाँदै हुनुहुन्छ।
āja rāta tapāīṃ tīna pr̥ṣṭha gr̥hakārya lekhna jā~dai hunuhuncha
He is going to sleep early because he has to catch the morning train.
बिहानको रेल समात्न उहाँ छिटो सुत्न जाँदै हुनुहुन्छ।
bihānako rela samātnā uhā~ chiṭo sutna jā~dai hunuhuncha
She is going to cook dal bhat for twenty guests in our kitchen.
हाम्रो भान्सामा बीस जनाका लागि उनी दाल भात पकाउन जाँदैछिन्।
hāmro bhānsāmā bīsa janākā lāgi unī dāla bhāta pakāuna jā~daichin
We are going to meet the head teacher at ten o'clock tomorrow.
भोलि दस बजे हामी प्रधानाध्यापक भेट्न जाँदैछौं।
bholi dasa baje hāmī pradhānādhyāpaka bheṭna jā~daichauṃ
I am going to buy vegetables at the market on my way home.
घर फर्कँदै म बजारमा तरकारी किन्न जाँदैछु।
ghara pharka~dai ma bajāramā tarkārī kinna jā~daichu
Tomorrow I am going to come with fresh fruit from our garden.
भोलि म आफ्नो बारीबाट ताजा फलफूल लिएर आउँदै छु।
bholi ma āphno barībāṭa tājā phalphūl liera āu~dai chu
She is going to sing the national anthem at the opening ceremony.
उद्घाटन समारोहमा उनी राष्ट्रिय गान गाउन लागेकी छिन्।
udghāṭana samārohamā unī rāṣṭriya gāna gāuna lāgekī chin
They are going to wait in the hall until the doctor calls them.
डाक्टरले बोलाउनुहुँदासम्म तिनीहरू हलमा पर्खन जाँदैछन्।
ḍākṭarale bolāunuhu~dāsamma tinīharū halamā parkhana jā~daichan

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase in Nepali
content: Past habit: "used to". Often past tense + habitual context.
intro: Habitual past: म जान्थें (I used to go), उ खान्थे (he used to eat), -थें/-थे/-थिन् endings.

blocks.heading: Used to phrase
rows: I used to walk to school with my elder brother every morning.
हरेक बिहान म ठूलो दाइसँग हिँडेर स्कुल जान्थें।
hareka bihāna ma ṭhūlo dāisa~ga hi~ḍera skula jāntheṃ
We used to visit our grandparents in the village during Dashain.
दशैँमा हामी गाउँमा हजुरबुबा हजुरआमालाई भेट्न जान्थ्यौं।
daśai~mā hāmī gāu~mā hajurabubā hajurāmālāī bheṭna jānthyauṃ
He used to play football in the empty field behind our house.
उ हाम्रो घर पछाडिको खाली मैदानमा फुटबल खेल्थे।
u hāmro ghara pachāḍiko khālī maidānamā phuṭabala khelthe
She used to read novels late at night under a small lamp.
उनी सानो बत्तीमुनि रात अबेरसम्म उपन्यास पढ्थिन्।
unī sāno battīmuni rāta aberasamma upanyāsa paḍhthin
They used to speak Maithili at home and Nepali outside.
उनीहरू घरमा मैथिली बोल्थे र बाहिर नेपाली।
unīharū gharamā maithilī bolthe ra bāhira nepālī
You used to help your mother in the kitchen on Sundays.
आइतबार तिमी आमालाई भान्सामा सहयोग गर्थ्यौ।
āitabāra timī āmālāī bhānsāmā sahayoga garthyau
I used to drink milk before sleeping when I was a child.
सानो हुँदा सुत्नुअघि म दुध पिउँथें।
sāno hu~dā sutnuaghi ma dudha piu~theṃ
We used to listen to the radio news together after dinner.
रातको खानापछि हामी सँगै रेडियो समाचार सुन्थ्यौं।
rātako khānapachi hāmī saṃgai reḍiyo samācāra sunthyauṃ
He used to work in a tea shop to pay for his books.
उ किताब किन्न चियापसलमा काम गर्थे।
u kitāba kinna ciyāpasalamā kāma garthe
She used to sing folk songs at every village fair.
उनी हरेक मेलामा लोक गीत गाउने गर्थिन्।
unī hareka melāmā loka gīta gāune garthin
I used to feel shy when strangers asked me questions in English.
We used to save coins in a clay piggy bank every month.
हरेक महिना हामी सिक्का माटोको गुल्लकमा जम्मा गर्थ्यौं।
hareka mahinā hāmī sikkā māṭoko gullakamā jamā garthyauṃ
They used to argue loudly but they never stopped being friends.
उनीहरू चर्को स्वरमा बहस गर्थे तर साथी भने रहन्थे।
unīharū carko svaramā bahasa garthe tara sāthī bhane rahanthe
You used to borrow my bicycle without asking on weekends.
छुट्टीमा तिमी नसोधी मेरो साइकल लिनुहुन्थ्यो।
chuṭṭīmā timī nasodhī mero sāikala linuhunthyo
I used to believe every story my grandfather told by the fire.
आगो नजिक बसेर हजुरबुबाले भनेका कथा म सबै सत्य मान्थें।
āgo najika basera hajurabubāle bhanekā kathā ma sabai satya māntheṃ

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase – old style in Nepali
content: Older or alternative way to express past habit.
intro: Same meaning with different verb form or particle.

blocks.heading: Used to (old style)
rows: I used to go there on foot when the road was still unpaved.
बाटो कच्ची हुँदा म त्यहाँ हिँडेर जान्थें।
bāṭo kaccī hu~dā ma tyahā~ hi~ḍera jāntheṃ
You used to say that hard work always brings good results.
तिमी भन्नुहुन्थ्यो मेहनतले राम्रो फल दिन्छ।
timī bhannuhunthyo mēhanatale rāmro phala dincha
He used to walk along the river bank before sunrise every day.
उ हरेक दिन घाम नउदाउँदै नदी किनार हिँड्थे।
u hareka dina ghāma naudāu~dai nadī kināra hi~ḍthe
She used to dance at local weddings when she was very young.
उनी धेरै सानो हुँदा गाउँका विवाहमा नाच्ने गर्थिन्।
unī dherai sāno hu~dā gāu~kā vivāhamā nācne garthin
We used to travel by night bus to reach Kathmandu cheaply.
सस्तोमा काठमाडौं पुग्न हामी रातको बसमा यात्रा गर्थ्यौं।
sastomā kāṭhamāḍauṃ pugna hāmī rātako basamā yātrā garthyauṃ
They used to visit us every Tihar with boxes of sweets.
तिहारमा उनीहरू मिठाइका डब्बा लिएर हामीलाई भेट्न आउँथे।
tihāramā unīharū miṭhāikā ḍabbā liera hāmīlāī bheṭna āu~the
I used to wake at five to milk the cow before school.
स्कुल जानुअघि पाँच बजे उठेर गाई दुहुन्थें।
skula jānuaghi pā̃ca baje uṭhera gāī duhu~theṃ
You used to sleep late after watching films on the neighbour's TV.
छिमेकीको टिभीमा चलचित्र हेरेर तिमी ढिलो सुत्नुहुन्थ्यो।
chimekīko ṭibīmā calacitra herera timī ḍhilo sutnuhunthyo
He used to drink hot water with honey when he caught a cold.
रुघा लाग्दा उ मधु मिसाएको तातो पानी पिउँथे।
rughā lāgdā u madhu misāeko tāto pānī piu~the
She used to help the shopkeeper count money at closing time.
पसल बन्द हुँदा उनी पसलेलाई पैसा गन्न सहयोग गर्थिन्।
pasala banda hu~dā unī pasalelāī paisā ganna sahayoga garthin
We used to go to the same school but we were not in the same class.
हामी एउटै स्कुल जान्थ्यौं तर एउटै कक्षामा पर्थेनौं।
hāmī euṭai skula jānthyauṃ tara euṭai kakṣāmā parthenauṃ
I used to like that old song though I did not know the words.
शब्द नबुझ्दा पनि त्यो पुरानो गीत मलाई मन पर्थ्यो।
śabda nabu-jhdā pani tyo purāno gīta malāī mana parthyo
They used to fight over toys and then share biscuits quietly.
खेलौनाका लागि झगडा गर्थे अनि चुपचाप बिस्कुट बाँड्थे।
khelaunākā lāgi jhagaḍā garthe ani cupcāpa biskuṭa bā~ḍthe
She used to cry easily but she also forgave people quickly.
उनी सजिलै रोइरहन्थिन् तर चाँडै माफ पनि गर्थिन्।
unī sajilai roirahanthin tara cā~ḍai māpha pani garthin
He used to laugh at my jokes even when they were not funny.
मेरा जोक हास्यास्पद नभए पनि ऊ हाँस्ने गर्दथ्यो।
merā joka hāsyāspada nabhae pani ū hā~sne gardathyo

title: Sentence using "If-Then" in Nepali
content: Conditional: If X then Y. जँ ... तँ, यदि ... त.
intro: If you come, I will go = तिमी आयौ भने म जान्छु / म जानेछु.

blocks.heading: If-Then sentences
rows: If you come before six, we can eat dinner together at home.
छ बजेभन्दा अघि आयौ भने हामी घरमै सँगै रातको खाना खान सक्छौं।
cha bajebhandā aghi āyau bhane hāmī gharamai saṃgai rātako khānā khāna sakkchauṃ
If it rains heavily, the match will move to the indoor hall.
धेरै पानी पर्यो भने खेल हल भित्र सर्नेछ।
dherai pānī paryo bhane khela halla bhitra sarnēcha
If he finishes the work today, the boss will pay him a bonus.
उसले आज काम सकायो भने मालिकले बोनस दिनेछ।
usale āja kāma sakāyo bhane mālikale bonasa dinēcha
If you want honest advice, ask your teacher directly.
इमानदार सल्लाह चाहनुहुन्छ भने शिक्षकलाई सिधै सोध्नुहोस्।
imāndāra sallāha cāhanuhuncha bhane śikṣakalāī sidhai sodhnuhos
If she runs faster in the last lap, she can still win the race.
अन्तिम चक्कामा छिटो दौडिन् भने उनी अझै जित्न सक्छिन्।
antima cakkāmā chiṭo dauḍin bhane unī ajhai jitna sakkchin
If we leave now, we will reach the station before the train departs.
अहिले निस्कियौं भने रेल छुट्नुअघि स्टेशन पुग्नेछौं।
ahile niskiyauṃ bhane rela chuṭnuaghi sṭeśana pugnechauṃ
If you speak slowly, I will understand every word clearly.
तिमी ढिलो बोल्छौ भने म हरेक शब्द स्पष्ट बुझ्छु।
timī ḍhilo bolchau bhane ma hareka śabda spaṣṭa bujh-chu
If the shop is closed, we will buy bread from the stall next door.
पसल बन्द भयो भने छिमेकीको पसलबाट रोटी किन्नेछौं।
pasala banda bhayo bhane chimekīko pasalabāṭa roṭī kinnechauṃ
If the medicine does not work, call the doctor again tonight.
औषधि काम नगर्यो भने आज रात फेरि डाक्टरलाई फोन गर्नुहोस्।
auṣadhi kāma nagaryo bhane āja rāta pheri ḍākṭaralāī phona garnuhos
If he arrives late, start the meeting without waiting for him.
उ ढिलो आए पनि बैठक उसको पर्खाइ नगरी सुरु गर्नुहोस्।
u ḍhilo āe pani baiṭhaka usako parkhāi nagarī suru garnuhos
If you need money for the ticket, tell me before Friday.
टिकटका लागि पैसा चाहियो भने शुक्रबारअघि भन्नुहोस्।
ṭikaṭakā lāgi paisā cāhiyo bhane śukrabārāghi bhannuhos
If today is a public holiday, the banks will stay closed.
आज सार्वजनिक बिदा हो भने बैंक बन्द रहनेछन्।
āja sārvajanika bidā ho bhane baiṅk banda rahanechan
If there is no sugar, I will drink plain black tea.
चिनी छैन भने म खाली कालो चिया पिउँछु।
cinī chaina bhane ma khālī kālo ciyā piu~chu
If possible, bring your identity card to the office tomorrow.
सम्भव भए भोलि कार्यालयमा परिचयपत्र ल्याउनुहोस्।
sambhava bhae bholi kāryālayamā paricayapatra lyāunuhos
If you understand this rule, explain it once to your partner.
यो नियम बुझ्नुभयो भने साथीलाई एक पटक बुझाउनुहोस्।
yo niyama bujhnubhayo bhane sāthīlāī eka paṭaka bujhāunuhos

title: Prepositions with Verbs in Nepali
content: Verbs that take a fixed postposition: लाग (need), चाह (want), सँ (from).
intro: Dependent on verb: जाब लागल (going to go), देखब लागल (going to see).

blocks.heading: Verbs with prepositions
rows: I am looking for a quiet corner to read without noise.
म शान्त कोठा खोजिरहेको छु जहाँ बिना हल्ला पढ्न सकूँ।
ma śānta koṭhā khoji-raheko chu jahā~ binā hallā paḍhna sakū~
She spoke with the manager about the delay in salary.
उनीले तलब ढिलो भएको बारेमा प्रबन्धकसँग कुरा गरिन्।
unīle talaba ḍhilo bhaeko bāremā prabandhakasa~ga kurā garin
We waited under the tree for the rain to stop completely.
पानी पूर्ण रूपले रोकिएसम्म हामी रुखमुनि पर्खियौं।
pānī pūrṇa rūpale rokiesamma hāmī rukhamuni parkhiyauṃ
He depends on his bicycle to reach the office on time.
समयमै कार्यालय पुग्न उहाँ साइकलमा भर पर्नुहुन्छ।
samayamai kāryālaya pugna uhā~ sāikalamā bhara parnuhuncha
They ran towards the gate when they heard the fire alarm.
आगोको साइरन सुन्दा उनीहरू गेटतर्फ दौडिए।
āgoko sāiran sundā unīharū geṭatarpha dauḍie
I believe in your honesty even when others doubt you.
अरूले शंका गर्दा पनि म तपाईंको इमानदारीमा विश्वास गर्छु।
arūle śaṅkā gardā pani ma tapāīṃko imāndārīmā viśvāsa gar-chu
Please sit on this chair; the floor is still wet from mopping.
यो कुर्सीमा बस्नुहोस्; पुछेर भुइँ अझै भिजेको छ।
yo kursīmā basnuhos; puchera bhui~ ajhai bhijeko cha
We live in a small flat near the ring road with two rooms.
हामी दुई कोठे सानो फ्ल्याटमा रिङ्गरोड नजिक बस्छौं।
hāmī duī koṭhe sāno phlyāṭamā riṅgaroḍa najika bas-chauṃ
She wrote the letter with a blue pen from her bag.
उनीले झोलाबाट नीलो कलमले पत्र लेखिन्।
unīle jholābāṭa nīlo kalamale patra lekhin
I cannot eat this curry without salt; it tastes completely flat.
नुन बिना यो तरकारी खान सक्दिन; स्वादै आउँदैन।
nuna binā yo tarkārī khāna sakdina; svādai āu~dain
He listened to the radio news while cooking in the kitchen.
उहाँ भान्सामा खाना पकाउँदै रेडियो समाचार सुन्नुहुन्थ्यो।
uhā~ bhānsāmā khānā pakāu~dai reḍiyo samācāra sunnuhunthyo
Think about the consequences before you sign this paper.
यो कागजमा दस्तखत गर्नुअघि परिणाम सोच्नुहोस्।
yo kāgajamā dastakhata garnuaghi pariṇāma socnuhos
I need to give this parcel to your sister before noon.
दिउँसो अघि यो पार्सल तपाईंकी बहिनीलाई दिनु पर्छ।
diuṃso aghi yo pārsala tapāīṃkī bahinīlāī dinu parcha
They came from Pokhara last night and look very tired.
उनीहरू हिजो रात पोखराबाट आएका छन् र थाकेका देखिन्छन्।
unīharū hijo rāta pokharābāṭa āekā chan ra thākekā dekhinchan
We will meet at the station and walk to the temple together.
हामी स्टेशनमा भेटेर सँगै मन्दिर हिँड्नेछौं।
hāmī sṭeśanamā bheṭera saṃgai mandira hi~ḍnechauṃ

title: Adjectives in Nepali
content: Adjectives describe nouns. नीक (good), पैघ (big), सुन्दर (beautiful).
intro: Optional agreement: सुन्दर/सुन्दरी. Usually before noun.

blocks.heading: Adjective examples
rows: The old wooden bridge looks dangerous after the last flood.
पछिल्लो बाढीपछि त्यो पुरानो काठको पुल खतरनाक देखिन्छ।
pachillo bāḍhīpachi tyo purāno kāṭhako pula khatarānāka dekhincha
She wore a bright red sari for her sister's wedding.
उनीले बहिनीको विवाहमा उज्यालो रातो सारी लगाइन्।
unīle bahinīko vivāhamā ujyālo rāto sārī lagāin
This small room is enough for one bed and a study table.
यो सानो कोठामा एउटा ओछ्यान र पढ्ने टेबुल पुग्छ।
yo sāno koṭhāmā euṭā ochyāna ra paḍhne ṭebula pugcha
The mountain air feels cold and clean early in the morning.
बिहानै पहाडको हावा चिसो र सफा लाग्छ।
bihānai pahāḍako hāvā ciso ra saphā lāgcha
He is a tall young man who plays basketball for the college team.
उ कलेज टिमका लागि बास्केटबल खेल्ने अग्लो युवा हो।
u kalej ṭimakā lāgi bāskēṭabala khelne aglo yuvā ho
We bought sweet juicy mangoes from the street vendor.
हामीले गल्लीको फलफूल पसलबाट गुलियो आँप किन्यौं।
hāmīle gallīko phalphūl pasalabāṭa guliyo ā~pa kinyauṃ
The soup is too salty; please add a little hot water.
सुप धेरै नुनिलो छ; अलि तातो पानी हाल्नुहोस्।
supa dherai nunilo cha; ali tāto pānī hālnuhos
Her voice is soft but everyone in the hall can hear her clearly.
उनको आवाज मधुरो छ तर हलका सबैले स्पष्ट सुन्छन्।
unako āvāja madhuro cha tara halakā sabaile spaṣṭa sunchan
This expensive watch was a gift from my uncle abroad.
यो महँगो घडी विदेशमा भएका काकाबाट उपहार हो।
yo mah~go ghaḍī videśamā bhaekā kākābāṭa upahāra ho
The path is narrow, so walk carefully with a torch at night.
बाटो साँघुरो छ, रातमा बत्ती बालेर सावधान हिँड्नुहोस्।
bāṭo sā~ghuro cha, rātamā battī bālera sāvadhāna hi~ḍnuhos
Fresh bread from the oven smells wonderful in the morning.
भट्टीबाट निस्केको ताजा रोटी बिहान अद्भुत सुगन्ध छ।
bhaṭṭībāṭa niskeko tāja roṭī bihān adbhuta sugandha cha
He looks tired because he worked a double shift yesterday.
उ थाकेको देखिन्छ किनभने हिजो उसले डबल शिफ्ट काम गर्यो।
u thākeko dekhincha kinabhane hijo usale ḍabala śiphaṭa kāma garyo
The baby is healthy and sleeps peacefully through the night.
बच्चा स्वस्थ छ र रातभरि शान्त सुत्छ।
baccā svastha cha ra rātābhari śānta sut-cha
That distant peak stays white with snow even in late spring.
ढिलो वसन्तमा पनि टाढाको चुचुरो हिउँले सेतो रहन्छ।
ḍhilo vasanta pani ṭāḍhāko cucuro hiu~le seto rahancha
We need a larger table because six guests are coming tonight.
आज रात छ जना आउँदैछन्, ठूलो टेबल चाहिन्छ।
āja rāta cha janā āu~daichan, ṭhūlo ṭebula cāhincha

title: Using verb "Can/To be able to" in Nepali
content: Ability: सक्नु + infinitive. म गर्न सक्छु (I can do).
intro: Can = सक्नु: म सक्छु, तपाईं सक्नुहुन्छ. Negative: सक्दिन / सक्नुहुन्न.

blocks.heading: Can / be able to
rows: I can swim across this river when the current is calm.
बेग शान्त हुँदा म यो नदी तर्न सक्छु।
bega śānta hu~dā ma yo nadī tarna sak-chu
You can leave early today if you finish the report by four.
चार बजेभित्र प्रतिवेदन सकाउनुभयो भने आज छिटो जान सक्नुहुन्छ।
cāra bajebhitra prativedana sakāunubhayo bhane āja chiṭo jāna saknuhuncha
He cannot lift this heavy box alone; let us help him.
उ एक्लैले यो भारी डब्बा उचाल्न सक्दैन; सहयोग गरौं।
u eklaile yo bhārī ḍabbā ucālna sakdaina; sahayoga garauṃ
She can speak three languages fluently in meetings.
उनी बैठकमा तीन भाषा धाराप्रवाह बोल्न सक्छिन्।
unī baiṭhakamā tīna bhāṣā dhārāpravāha bolna sakkchin
We can hear the temple bells from our roof on quiet nights.
शान्त रातमा हामी छानाबाट मन्दिरको घन्टी सुन्न सक्छौं।
śānta rātamā hāmī chānābāṭa mandirako ghaṇṭī sunna sakkchauṃ
They cannot enter the hall without a printed ticket.
छापिएको टिकट बिना उनीहरू हल भित्र पस्न सक्दैनन्।
chāpieko ṭikaṭa binā unīharū halla bhitra pasna sakdainan
I could not sleep last night because of the loud music next door.
छिमेकीको ठूलो संगीतले गर्दा हिजो रात म सुत्न सकिनँ।
chimekīko ṭhūlo saṅgītale gardā hijo rāta ma sutna sakiñ
Can you read this small print without glasses?
तपाईंले यो सानो अक्षर चश्मा बिना पढ्न सक्नुहुन्छ?
tapāīṃle yo sāno akṣara caśmā binā paḍhna saknuhuncha
He can drive a truck but he prefers a motorbike in traffic.
उ ट्रक चलाउन सक्छ तर भीडमा मोटरसाइकल रुचाउँछ।
u ṭraka calāuna sakcha tara bhīḍamā moṭarasāikala rucāu~cha
She could not answer the phone while she was in the shower.
We can meet next week if your schedule is too tight now.
अहिले समय छैन भने अर्को हप्ता भेट्न सक्छौं।
ahile samaya chaina bhane arko haptā bheṭna sakkchauṃ
I can smell smoke; please check the kitchen immediately.
धुवाँको गन्ध आउँछ; तुरुन्त भान्सा हेर्नुहोस्।
dhuvā~ko gandha āu~cha; turunta bhānsā hernuhos
You cannot board the bus once the doors have closed.
ढोका बन्द भएपछि तपाईं बसमा चढ्न सक्नुहुन्न।
ḍhokā banda bhaepachi tapāīṃ basamā chaḍhna saknunn
He can fix this tap in five minutes if he has the right tool.
सही औजार भए पाँच मिनेटमै उसले यो ट्याप मर्मत गर्न सक्छ।
sahī aujāra bhae pā̃ca mineṭamai usale yo ṭyāpa marmata garna sakcha
They can stay with us until they find a cheaper flat.
सस्तो फ्ल्याट नपाउँदासम्म उनीहरू हामीसँग बस्न सक्छन्।
sasto phlyāṭa napāu~dāsamma unīharū hāmīsa~ga basna sakkchan

title: Using verb To Want/To Need in Nepali
content: Want = चाही, चाहब. Need = जरूरी, आवश्यकता.
intro: मलाई चाहिन्छ (I want / need). तपाईंलाई आवश्यक छ (you need).

blocks.heading: Want / Need
rows: I need a glass of water before I take this medicine.
यो औषधि खानुअघि मलाई एक गिलास पानी चाहिन्छ।
yo auṣadhi khānuaghi malāī eka gilāsa pānī cāhincha
You want to rest, but the guests will arrive in ten minutes.
तपाईं आराम चाहनुहुन्छ, तर दस मिनेटमै पाहुना आउँदैछन्।
tapāīṃ ārāma cāhanuhuncha, tara dasa mineṭamai pāhunā āu~daichan
He needs permission from the principal to leave the campus.
क्याम्पस छोड्न उहाँलाई प्रधानाध्यापकको अनुमति चाहिन्छ।
kyāmpasa choḍnu uhā~lāī pradhānādhyāpakako anumati cāhincha
She wants to study abroad next year if she passes the exam.
परीक्षा पास भए अर्को वर्ष विदेश पढ्न उनी चाहन्छिन्।
parīkṣā pāsa bhae arko varṣa videśa paḍhna unī cāhanchin
We need rice, lentils, oil, and spices from the market today.
आज बजारबाट चामल, दाल, तेल र मसला चाहिन्छ।
āja bajārabāṭa cāmala, dāla, tela ra masalā cāhincha
They do not want any sugar in their tea this morning.
I wanted to call you yesterday but my phone battery died.
हिजो तपाईंलाई फोन गर्न चाहेको थिएँ तर ब्याट्री सकियो।
hijo tapāīṃlāī phona garna cāheko thie~ tara byāṭrī sakiyo
You need warm clothes if you are going to the hills in winter.
जाडोमा पहाड जानुहुन्छ भने न्यानो लुगा चाहिन्छ।
jāḍomā pahāḍ jānuhuncha bhane nyāno lugā cāhincha
He wants honest feedback even when the news is bad.
खबर नराम्रो भए पनि उ सत्य प्रतिक्रिया चाहन्छ।
khabara narāmro bhae pani u satya pratikriyā cāhancha
She needs someone to stay with the baby while she goes shopping.
We want to plant trees along this road before the monsoon.
मनसुनअघि यो बाटोमा रुख रोप्न हामी चाहन्छौं।
manasunaghi yo bāṭomā rukha ropna hāmī cāhanchauṃ
I need your signature here to open a joint bank account.
संयुक्त बैंक खाता खोल्न यहाँ तपाईंको दस्तखत चाहिन्छ।
saṃyukta baiṅk khātā kholna yahā~ tapāīṃko dastakhata cāhincha
You wanted to become a doctor when you were in school.
स्कुल हुँदा तपाईं डाक्टर बन्न चाहनुहुन्थ्यो।
skula hu~dā tapāīṃ ḍākṭara banna cāhanuhunthyo
He needs a day off because his mother is in hospital.
आमा अस्पतालमा भएकाले उसलाई एक दिन बिदा चाहिन्छ।
āmā aspatālamā bhaekāle usalāī eka dina bidā cāhincha
They want the meeting to start on time without long speeches.
लामो भाषण बिना समयमै बैठक सुरु हुन उनीहरू चाहन्छन्।
lāmo bhaṣaṇa binā samayamai baiṭhaka suru huna unīharū cāhanchan

title: Verb "To Become/To Happen" in Nepali
content: To become = भ होब. To happen = होइत, भ होइत.
intro: He became rich = ओ अमीर भ गेल. What happened? = की भेल?

blocks.heading: Become / Happen
rows: The sky became dark in minutes before the storm arrived.
आँधी आउनु अघि आकाश केही मिनेटमै अँध्यारो भयो।
ā~dhī āunu aghi ākāśa kehī mineṭamai a~dhyāro bhayo
She became a nurse after years of training in the city hospital.
सहरको अस्पतालमा वर्षौं तालिम पछि उनी नर्स बनिन्।
saharako aspatālamā varṣauṃ tālima pachi unī narsa banin
He will become an engineer if he passes these tough exams.
कडा परीक्षा पास भए उ इन्जिनियर बन्नेछ।
kaḍā parīkṣā pāsa bhae u injiniyara bannēcha
The milk turned sour because we forgot to put it in the fridge.
What happened after the lights went out in the hall?
Nothing serious happened; it was only a short power cut.
गम्भीर केही भएन; छोटो लोडसेडिङ मात्र थियो।
gambhīra kehi bhaena; choṭo loḍaseḍiṅ mātra thiyo
The child became quiet when the teacher entered the room.
शिक्षक कोठामा पस्दा बच्चा शान्त भयो।
śikṣaka koṭhāmā pasdā baccā śānta bhayo
We became good friends while working on the same project.
एउटै परियोजनामा काम गर्दा हामी राम्रो साथी बन्यौं।
euṭai pariyojanāmā kāma gardā hāmī rāmro sāthī banyauṃ
The road became slippery after the light drizzle.
हल्का पानी परेपछि बाटो चिप्लो भयो।
halkā pānī parepachi bāṭo ciplo bhayo
He became angry only when they insulted his parents.
आमाबुबालाई अपमान गर्दा मात्र उ रिसायो।
āmābubālāī apamāna gardā mātra u risāyo
She became famous in the village after winning the singing prize.
गीत प्रतियोगिता जितेपछि उनी गाउँमा चर्चित भइन्।
gīta pratiyogitā jitepachi unī gāu~mā carcita bhain
It may become colder tonight, so take a jacket.
आज रात अझ चिसो हुन सक्छ, ज्याकेट लिनुहोस्।
āja rāta ajha ciso huna sakcha, jyākeṭa linuhos
The discussion became a loud argument within seconds.
केही सेकेण्डमै छलफल ठूलो बहस बन्यो।
kehī sekeṇḍamai chalaphala ṭhūlo bahasa banyo
I became more patient after I started teaching young children.
साना बच्चा पढाउन थालेपछि म धैर्यवान भएँ।
sānā baccā paḍhāuna thālepachi ma dhairyavāna bhae~
They became neighbours when we moved to this street last year.
गत वर्ष यो गल्ली सरेपछि उनीहरू छिमेकी बने।
gata varṣa yo gallī sarepachi unīharū chimekī bane

title: Using "Should" in Nepali
content: Should = चाही, करबाक चाही. Advice or obligation.
intro: You should go = तपाईं जानुपर्छ / जानुहोस्. I should eat = मैले खानुपर्छ.

blocks.heading: Should
rows: You should wear a helmet when you ride a motorbike in traffic.
We should save a little money every month for emergencies.
आपत्कालिन खर्चका लागि हरेक महिना अलि पैसा जम्मा गर्नुपर्छ।
āpatkālina kharccakā lāgi hareka mahinā ali paisā jamā garnuparcha
He should apologise if he spoke rudely to the elder.
जेठोलाई असभ्य बोलेको भए माफी माग्नुपर्छ।
jeṭholāī asabhya boleko bhae māphī māgnu parcha
She should rest for two days because the fever is still high.
ज्वरो अझै छ भने दुई दिन आराम गर्नुपर्छ।
jvaro ajhai cha bhane duī dina ārāma garnuparcha
I should have called you earlier; I am sorry for the delay.
अघि नै फोन गर्नुपर्थ्यो; ढिलो भएकोमा माफ।
aghi nai phona garnuparthyo; ḍhilo bhaekomā māpha
They should lock the gate at night for everyone's safety.
सबको सुरक्षाका लागि राति गेट बन्द गर्नुपर्छ।
sabako surakṣākā lāgi rāti geṭa banda garnuparcha
You should not eat street food if your stomach is sensitive.
पेट नाजुक छ भने गल्लीको खाना खानु हुँदैन।
peṭa nājuka cha bhane gallīko khānā khānu hu~dain
We should plant more trees along this dry river bed.
यो सुक्खा नदी किनारमा थप रुख रोप्नुपर्छ।
yo sukkhā nadī kināramā thapa rukha ropnu parcha
He should read the instructions before switching on the machine.
mesina calāunu aghi nirdēśana paḍhnu parcha
She should carry water while hiking under the hot sun.
garimāmā hi~ḍdā pānī sātha lānu parcha
I should leave now or I will miss the last bus home.
अब ननिस्किए घर जाने अन्तिम बस छुट्छ।
aba naniskie ghara jāne antima basa chuṭcha
You should tell the truth even when it is uncomfortable.
We should check the tyre pressure before a long journey.
लामो यात्राअघि टायरको हावा जाँच गर्नुपर्छ।
lāmo yātrāaghi ṭāyarako hāvā jā̃ca garnuparcha
They should not make noise after ten at night in this colony.
यो बस्तीमा राति दस पछि हल्ला गर्नु हुँदैन।
yo bastīmā rāti dasa pachi hallā garnu hu~dain
You should thank everyone who helped you during the crisis.
संकटमा सहयोग गर्ने सबैलाई धन्यवाद भन्नुपर्छ।
saṅkaṭamā sahayoga garne sabailāī dhanyavāda bhannuparcha

title: Using "Must"/"to be have to" in Nepali
content: Must / have to = अवश्य, जरूरी, लागल. Strong obligation.
intro: I must go = म जानैपर्छ. You have to come = तपाईं आउनुपर्छ.

blocks.heading: Must / Have to
rows: I must submit this form before five or the office will reject it.
पाँच बजेभन्दा अघि यो फारम बुझाउनु पर्छ नत्र अस्वीकृत हुन्छ।
pā̃ca bajebhandā aghi yo phārama bujhāunu parcha natra asvīkṛta huncha
You have to show your ticket at the gate; there is no other way.
गेटमा टिकट देखाउनु नै पर्छ; अर्को उपाय छैन।
geṭamā ṭikaṭa dekhāunu nai parcha; arko upāya chaina
He has to wake at four every day to catch the milk van.
दुधको गाडी समात्न हरेक दिन चार बजे उठ्नु पर्छ।
dudhako gāḍī samātnā hareka dina cāra baje uṭhnu parcha
We must wear shoes inside the factory for safety rules.
कारखानाभित्र सुरक्षा नियमअनुसार जुत्ता लगाउनु पर्छ।
kārakhanābhitra surakṣā niyamānusāra juttā lagāunu parcha
They have to finish harvesting before the heavy rain starts.
धेरै पानी नपर्दै कटनी सकाउनु पर्छ।
dherai pānī napardai kaṭanī sakāunu parcha
I had to walk home because my bicycle had a flat tyre.
साइकलको टायर पंचर भएकाले घर हिँडेर जानु पर्यो।
sāikalako ṭāyara pañcara bhaekāle ghara hi~ḍera jānu paryo
You must not smoke here; this is a hospital zone.
यहाँ धूम्रपान गर्न हुँदैन; अस्पताल क्षेत्र हो।
yahā~ dhūmrapāna garnu hu~dain; aspatāla kṣetra ho
She has to translate this letter by tomorrow morning.
भोलि बिहानसम्म यो पत्र अनुवाद गर्नु पर्छ।
bholi bihānasamma yo patra anuvāda garnu parcha
We must inform the police if we see anything suspicious.
शंकास्पद देखियो भने प्रहरीलाई खबर गर्नु पर्छ।
śaṅkāspada dekhiyo bhane praharīlāī khabara garnu parcha
He has to pay the fine because he parked on the wrong side.
गलत ठाउँमा गाडी राखेकाले जरिवाना तिर्नु पर्छ।
galata ṭhāu~mā gāḍī rākhekāle jarivānā tirnu parcha
I must remember to carry my umbrella; the forecast says rain.
छाता बिर्सनु हुँदैन; पानी पर्ने पूर्वानुमान छ।
chātā birsanu hu~dain; pānī parne pūrvānumāna cha
You have to sign in blue ink where the arrow points.
तीर जहाँ देखाउँछ त्यहाँ नीलो स्याहीले दस्तखत गर्नु पर्छ।
tīra jahā~ dekhāu~cha tyahā~ nīlo syāhile dastakhata garnu parcha
We must not waste drinking water while washing the car.
गाडी धुँदा पिउने पानी बर्बाद गर्न हुँदैन।
gāḍī dhu~dā piune pānī barabāda garnu hu~dain
They have to attend training on Saturday; attendance is compulsory.
शनिबार तालिम अनिवार्य छ, उपस्थिति जरूरी छ।
śanibāra tālima anivārya cha, upasthiti jarūrī cha
I have to leave now or I will miss my connecting flight.

title: Using phrase "to keep doing" in Nepali
content: Keep doing: -इरहनु / -दै जानु patterns for continuous or persistent action.
intro: Examples: खाइरहन्छ (keeps eating), दौडिरहन्छ (keeps running), जारी राख्नु (keep on).

blocks.heading: Keep doing
rows: The baby kept crying until the mother picked her up.
He kept working late every night to finish the project on time.
समयमै परियोजना सकाउन उ हरेक रात ढिलो काम गरिरह्यो।
samayamai pariyojana sakāuna u hareka rāta ḍhilo kāma garirahyo
She kept asking the same question until she understood the answer.
जवाफ नबुझ्दासम्म उनी एउटै प्रश्न सोधिरहिन्।
javāpha nabu-jhdāsamma unī euṭai praśna sodhirahin
We kept walking though our legs were tired and heavy.
They kept calling my phone while I was in the meeting.
बैठकमा हुँदा उनीहरूले मेरो फोन बारम्बार गरे।
baiṭhakamā hu~dā unīharūle mero phona bārambāra gare
The dog kept barking at strangers until the owner shouted at it.
I kept practising the song until I could sing it without mistakes.
You kept forgetting to lock the door when you left in a hurry.
हतारमा हुँदा तपाईं ढोका बन्द गर्न बिर्सनुभयो।
hatāramā hu~dā tapāīṃ ḍhokā banda garna birsanubhayo
The fan kept making noise so we switched it off at night.
पंखाले हल्ला गरिरह्यो, राति नै बन्द गर्यौं।
paṅkhāle hallā garirahyo, rāti nai banda garyauṃ
She kept stirring the milk so it would not stick to the pan.
कडाईमा दुध टाँसिन नदिन उनी हल्लाइरहिन्।
ḍaḍāimā dudha ṭā̃sina nadina unī hallāirin
We kept hoping for news though the phone stayed silent.
फोन नबजे पनि खबरको आशा गरिरह्यौं।
phona najage pani khabarako āśā garirahyauṃ
He kept checking the clock because the train was late.
रेल ढिलो भएकाले उ घडी हेरिरह्यो।
rel ḍhilo bhaekāle u ghaḍī herirahyo
The children kept running in circles until they were breathless.
सास फेर्न नसकुञ्जेल बच्चाहरू गोलगोल दौडिरहे।
sāsa pherna nasaku~jela baccāharū golagola dauḍirahe
I kept my promise and arrived exactly at seven.
सात बजे ठ्याक्कै आउने वाचा पूरा गरें।
sāta baje ṭhyākkai āune vācā pūrā gare~
You should rest; you have been coughing all night.

title: Comparison and degrees of comparison in Nepali
content: Comparative: धेरै, भन्दा ठूलो, अझै. Superlative: सबभन्दा, सबैभन्दा ठूलो.
intro: Bigger = ठूलो भन्दा ठूलो. Biggest = सबभन्दा ठूलो.

blocks.heading: Comparison
rows: This hill is higher than that small temple on the ridge.
यो डाँडो त्यो सानो मन्दिरभन्दा अग्लो छ।
yo ḍā~ḍo tyo sāno mandirabhandā aglo cha
My brother is taller than me but younger by two years.
मेरो भाइ मभन्दा अग्लो छ तर दुई वर्ष जुनियर।
mero bhāi mabhandā aglo cha tara duī varṣa juniyara
This winter feels colder than last winter in this valley.
यो उपत्यकामा यो जाडो गत वर्षभन्दा चिसो छ।
yo upatyakāmā yo jāḍo gata varṣabhandā ciso cha
She speaks more softly than anyone else in our family.
हाम्रो परिवारमा उनी अरूभन्दा मधुरो बोल्छिन्।
hāmro parivāramā unī arūbhandā madhuro bolchin
This road is the shortest route to the bus park.
बसपार्क जाने सबैभन्दा छोटो बाटो यही हो।
basapārka jāne sabaibhanda choṭo bāṭo yahī ho
He is the oldest student in our evening class this year.
यो वर्ष साँझको कक्षामा उ सबैभन्दा जेठो विद्यार्थी हो।
yo varṣa sā~jhako kakṣāmā u sabaibhanda jeṭho vidyārthī ho
This mango is sweeter than the ones we bought yesterday.
यो आँप हिजो किनेकोभन्दा गुलियो छ।
yo ā~pa hijo kinekobhandā guliyo cha
The second offer was better than the first one they showed us.
She runs faster now than she did before the training.
तालिम अघिभन्दा अहिले उ छिटो दौड्छिन्।
tālima aghibhandā ahile u chiṭo dauḍchin
This room is as big as the one we saw online in the photos.
यो कोठा अनलाइन फोटोमा देखेजत्तिकै ठूलो छ।
yo koṭhā analāina phoṭomā dekhejattikai ṭhūlo cha
I am not as patient as my grandmother when children make noise.
बच्चाले हल्ला गर्दा म हजुरआमाजत्तिको धैर्यवान छैन।
baccāle hallā gardā ma hajurāmājattiko dhairyavāna chaina
This tea is less bitter than the tea we drank at the station.
यो चिया स्टेशनकोभन्दा कम तितो छ।
yo ciyā sṭeśanakobhandā kama tito cha
He earns more than his cousin but spends more carefully.
उ आफ्नो भिनाजुभन्दा बढी कमाउँछ तर सजग खर्च गर्छ।
u āphno bhinājubhandā baḍhī kamāu~cha tara sajaga kharca gar-cha
The blue bag is the heaviest among these three suitcases.
तीन सुटकेसमध्ये नीलो झोला सबैभन्दा गह्रौँ छ।
tīna suṭakesamadhye nīlo jholā sabaibhanda gahrau~ cha
Walking is healthier than sitting all day in front of a screen.
दिनभरि स्क्रिन अगाडि बस्नुभन्दा हिँड्नु स्वास्थ्यको लागि राम्रो।
dinabhari skrina agāḍi basnubhandā hiḍnu svāsthryakō lāgi rāmro

title: Using verb "To Know" in Nepali
content: To know = थाहा हुनु, जान्नु, चिन्नु (people). मलाई थाहा छ (I know).
intro: Facts: थाहा छ. People: चिन्नु. Skill: ... गर्न जान्नु.

blocks.heading: To know
rows: I know your cousin; we studied in the same college.
म तपाईंको भिनाजुलाई चिन्छु; हामी एउटै कलेजमा पढेका हौं।
ma tapāīṃko bhinājulāī chin-chu; hāmī euṭai kalejamā paḍhekā hauṃ
Do you know how to send money through this mobile app?
यो मोबाइल एपबाट पैसा पठाउने तरिका थाहा छ?
yo mobāila epabāṭa paisā paṭhāune tarikā thāhā cha
She knows three local recipes that use only seasonal vegetables.
उनलाई मौसमी तरकारी मात्र प्रयोग गर्ने तीन स्थानीय विधि थाहा छ।
unalāī mausamī tarkārī mātra prayog garne tīna sthānīya vidhi thāhā cha
We knew the road was closed but we still tried a shortcut.
बाटो बन्द छ भन्ने थाहा थियो तर हामीले छोटो बाटो जोतियौं।
bāṭo banda cha bhannē thāhā thiyo tara hāmīle choṭo bāṭo jotiyauṃ
He does not know yet whether the interview will be online or in person.
अन्तर्वार्ता अनलाइन कि अनुहारअनुहार हुने थाहा छैन।
antarvārtā analāina ki anuhāraanuhāra hune thāhā chaina
I want to know the truth before I sign this paper.
यो कागजमा दस्तखत गर्नुअघि सत्य थाहा पाउन चाहन्छु।
yo kāgajamā dastakhata garnuaghi satya thāhā pāuna cāhanchu
You should know that we cannot wait forever for your reply.
हामी सधैं पर्खन सक्दैनौं भन्ने थाहा हुनुपर्छ।
hāmī sadhaiṃ parkhana sakdainauṃ bhannē thāhā hunuparcha
Nobody knew the thief would return to the same shop at night.
चोर राति उही पसल फर्कन्छ भन्ने कसैलाई थाहा थिएन।
cora rāti uhī pasala pharkancha bhannē kasailāī thāhā thiena
She knows every neighbour by name in this small lane.
यो साँघुरो गल्लीमा उनी हरेक छिमेकीको नाम जान्छिन्।
yo sā~ghuro gallīmā unī hareka chimekīko nāma jān-chin
I knew him as a quiet boy long before he became famous.
उ चर्चित हुनुअघि नै म उसलाई शान्त बच्चाको रूपमा चिन्थें।
u carcita hunuaghi nai ma usalāī śānta baccāko rūpamā chintheṃ
We will know the results only after the official announcement.
आधिकारिक घोषणापछि मात्र नतिजा थाहा हुन्छ।
ādhikārika ghoṣaṇāpachi mātra natijā thāhā huncha
He knows how to repair this old radio without a manual.
उसलाई म्यानुअल बिना यो पुरानो रेडियो मर्मत गर्न आउँछ।
usalāī myānuala binā yo purāno reḍiyo marmata garna āu~cha
Do you know where they sell fresh paneer in this market?
यो बजारमा ताजा पनीर कहाँ पाइन्छ थाहा छ?
yo bajāramā tāja panīra kahā~ pāincha thāhā cha
I did not know you could sing so well in Nepali.
तपाईं नेपालीमा यसरी राम्रो गाउन सक्नुहुन्छ भन्ने थाहा थिएन।
tapāīṃ nepālīmā yasarī rāmro gāuna saknuhuncha bhannē thāhā thiena
She knows the answer but she wants others to think first.
उनलाई जवाफ थाहा छ तर अरूले पहिले सोचुन् भन्छिन्।
unalāī javāpha thāhā cha tara arūle pahile socun bhanchin

title: Using "Let" and "Shall we" in Nepali
content: Let = दिनुहोस् / लै (allow). Suggestions: ... गरौँ?, ... हुन्छ?
intro: Let me go = मलाई जान दिनुहोस्. Shall we eat? = हामी खाऊँ?

blocks.heading: Let / Shall we
rows: Let us sit here for a minute before we climb the stairs.
पखेटा चढ्नुअघि एक मिनेट यहाँ बसौं।
pakheṭā chaḍhnuaghi eka mineṭa yahā~ basauṃ
Let him explain his idea; we should listen without interrupting.
उसले आफ्नो विचार बताओस्; बीचमा नबोली सुनौं।
usale āphno vicāra batāos; bīcamā nabolī sunauṃ
Let the children play outside until the street lights turn on.
गल्लीको बत्ती नबल्जेल बच्चाहरू बाहिर खेलून्।
gallīko battī nabaljela baccāharū bāhira khelūn
Shall we order two plates of momo and one plate of chow mein?
दुई प्लेट मोमो र एउटा चाउमिन अर्डर गरौं?
duī pleṭa momo ra euṭa cāumina arḍara garauṃ
Shall we meet at the station gate or in front of the tea stall?
स्टेशन गेटमा कि चियापसल अगाडि भेटौं?
sṭeśana geṭamā ki ciyāpasala agāḍi bheṭauṃ
Let us share a taxi; it will be cheaper for both of us.
ट्याक्सी साझा गरौं; दुवैलाई सस्तो पर्छ।
ṭyāksī sājhā garauṃ; duvaīlāī sasto parcha
Do not let the dog run on the road without a leash.
कुकुरलाई पटुका बिना सडकमा दौडाउनु हुँदैन।
kukuralāī paṭukā binā saḍakamā dauḍāunu hu~dain
Let her rest; she has been cooking since early morning.
उनलाई आराम गर्न दिऔं; बिहानैदेखि भान्सामा छिन्।
unalāī ārāma garna di auṃ; bihānaidekhi bhānsāmā chin
Shall we take the shorter path through the fields?
Let us call the electrician before we touch any wire.
तार छुनुअघि विद्युत मर्मतवालालाई फोन गरौं।
tāra chunuaghi vidyuta marmatavālālāī phona garauṃ
Let them decide; it is their money and their risk.
उनीहरूले निर्णय गरून्; उनीहरूको पैसा र जोखिम।
unīharūle nirṇaya garūn; unīharūko paisā ra jokhima
Shall we leave at six so we can avoid the morning traffic?
बिहानको जाम नटार्न छ बजे निस्कौं?
bihānako jāma naṭārna cha baje niskauṃ
Let me carry the heavy bag; your shoulder is still sore.
भारी झोला म बोकौं; तपाईंको काँधा अझै दुख्छ।
bhārī jholā ma bokauṃ; tapāīṃko kā~dhā ajhai duk-cha
Let us not argue in front of the guests tonight.
आज रात पाहुनाको अगाडि नबहस गरौं।
āja rāta pāhunāko agāḍi nabahasa garauṃ
Shall we ask the waiter for extra napkins and water?
अतिरिक्त टिस्यु र पानी वेटरलाई मागौं?
atirikta ṭisyu ra pānī veṭaralāī māgauṃ

title: Which-That / What-That sentences in Nepali
content: Relative: जे (that/which/who), जेकर (whose). जे बात (the thing that).
intro: The book that I read = मैले पढेको किताब. What you said = तपाईंले भनेको कुरा.

blocks.heading: Which / That / What
rows: The book that you lent me is more interesting than I expected.
तपाईंले दिनुभएको किताब मैले सोचेभन्दा रोचक छ।
tapāīṃle dinubhaeko kitāba maile sochebhandā rocaka cha
I remember the day when we first met at the bus stop.
बस स्टपमा पहिलो पटक भेटेको दिन मलाई सम्झना छ।
bas sṭapamā pahila paṭaka bheṭeko dina malāī sam̃jhānā cha
Tell me what you want for your birthday this year.
यो वर्ष जन्मदिनमा के चाहनुहुन्छ भन्नुहोस्।
yo varṣa janmadinamā ke cāhanuhuncha bhannuhos
This is the house where my grandparents lived for forty years.
यही घर हो जहाँ मेरा हजुरबुबा हजुरआमाले चालीस वर्ष बस्नुभयो।
yahī ghara ho jahā~ merā hajurabubā hajurāmāle cālīsa varṣa basnubhayo
The reason why he resigned is still not clear to anyone.
उसले राजिनामा किन दिए भन्ने अझै कसैलाई स्पष्ट छैन।
usale rājināmā kina die bhannē ajhai kasailāī spaṣṭa chaina
I do not know which bus goes directly to the airport.
विमानस्थल सिधै जाने कुन बस हो थाहा छैन।
vimānasthala sidhai jāne kuna basa ho thāhā chaina
She showed me how she folds the dough for perfect momos.
उनले मोमोको लागि पिठो कसरी मोड्ने देखाइन्।
unale momoko lāgi piṭho kasarī moḍne dekhāin
That is the man whose bicycle was stolen near the bridge.
पुल नजिक साइकल चोरी भएको मान्छे उही हो।
pula najika sāikala corī bhaeko mānche uhī ho
Whatever you decide, I will support you in public.
जे निर्णय गर्नुहुन्छ, सार्वजनिक रूपमा साथ दिन्छु।
je nirṇaya garnuhuncha, sārvajanika rūpamā sātha dinchu
The news that spread yesterday was only half true.
हिजो फैलिएको खबर आधा मात्र सत्य थियो।
hijo phailieko khabara ādhā mātra satya thiyo
I need a pen that writes smoothly on this rough paper.
यो खराब कागजमा राम्रो लेख्ने कलम चाहिन्छ।
yo kharāba kāgajamā rāmro lekhne kalama cāhincha
Come to the place where we usually drink tea on Sundays.
She understood what the officer meant without using a dictionary.
शब्दकोश नचलाई अधिकारीको मतलब उनले बुझिन्।
śabdakośa nacalāī adhikārīko matalaba unale bujhin
The story which he told made everyone laugh loudly.
उसले भनेको कथा सुनाएर सबै जोरले हाँसे।
usale bhaneko kathā sunāera sabai jorle hā~se
I will buy whichever shirt fits me best in this shop.
यो पसलमा जुन सर्ट मलाई मिल्छ त्यही किन्छु।
yo pasalamā juna saṭa malāī milcha tyahī kin-chu

title: Giving instructions formally in Nepali
content: Formal instructions: कृपया (please), अवश्य (must). Polite imperative.
intro: Please come = कृपया आउनुहोस्. You must submit = तपाईंले जम्मा गर्नुपर्छ.

blocks.heading: Formal instructions
rows: Please enter the room after knocking on the door twice.
ढोकामा दुई पटक ढकढक्याएर कोठामा पस्नुहोस्।
ḍhokāmā duī paṭaka ḍhakaḍhakyāera koṭhāmā pasnuhos
Kindly switch off your mobile phone during the examination.
Please submit the form at counter number three before noon.
दिउँसो अघि तेस्रो काउन्टरमा फारम बुझाउनुहोस्।
diuṃso aghi tesro kāuṇṭaramā phārama bujhāunuhos
You are requested to queue in a straight line and wait calmly.
सिधा लाइनमा बसेर शान्त रूपले पर्खनुहोला।
sidhā lāainamā basera śānta rūpale parkhanuholā
Please do not smoke within ten metres of this building.
यो भवनबाट दस मिटर भित्र धूम्रपान नगर्नुहोला।
yo bhavanabāṭa dasa miṭara bhitra dhūmrapāna nagarnuholā
Kindly show your identity card when the guard asks.
Please sign here and print your full name below.
यहाँ दस्तखत गरी तल पूरा नाम छाप्नुहोस्।
yahā~ dastakhata garī tala pūrā nāma chāpnuhos
You are advised to keep valuables in the locker provided.
महत्त्वपूर्ण सामान लकरमा राख्न अनुरोध छ।
mahattvapūrṇa samāna lakaramā rākhna anurodha cha
Please read the notice on the board before entering the lab.
Kindly maintain silence while others are speaking.
Please return the library books before the due date.
You are required to wear a mask in this crowded hall.
यो भीड भएको हलमा मास्क लगाउनु पर्छ।
yo bhīḍa bhaeko halamā māska lagāunu parcha
Please take a token and wait until your number appears on the screen.
टोकन लिई स्क्रिनमा नम्बर नआउँदासम्म पर्खनुहोस्।
ṭokana liī skrinamā nambara nāu~dāsamma parkhanuhos
Kindly inform the reception if you change your contact number.
सम्पर्क नम्बर बदल्नुभयो भने रिसेप्सनलाई जानकारी दिनुहोस्।
samparka nambara badalnubhayo bhane risepsanalāī jānkārī dinuhos
Please step back from the edge of the platform for your safety.
सुरक्षाका लागि प्लेटफर्मको छेउबाट अलि पछि हट्नुहोस्।
surakṣākā lāgi pleṭapharmako cheubāṭa ali pachi haṭnuhos

title: Using "To Like" in Nepali
content: To like = मन पर्नु, मन लाग्नु. Example: मलाई मन पर्छ (I like it).
intro: मलाई ... मन पर्छ. I like you = मलाई तिमी मन पर्छ / म तिमीलाई माया गर्छु.

blocks.heading: To like
rows: I like this quiet lane more than the noisy main road.
कोलाहलपूर्ण मुख्य सडकभन्दा मलाई यो शान्त गल्ली मन पर्छ।
kolahalapūrṇa mukhya saḍakabhandā malāī yo śānta gallī mana parcha
She likes spicy food but her doctor told her to reduce chilli.
पिरो मन पर्छ तर डाक्टरले खुर्सानी कम खान भन्नुभयो।
piro mana parcha tara ḍākṭarale khursānī kama khāna bhannubhayo
We like walking in the hills when the weather is clear.
मौसम खुला हुँदा पहाडमा हिँड्न मन लाग्छ।
mausama khulā hu~dā pahāḍamā hiḍna mana lāgcha
He does not like crowded buses so he leaves home early.
उलाई भीड भएको बस मन पर्दैन, त्यसैले छिटो निस्कन्छ।
ulāī bhīḍa bhaeko basa mana pardaina, tyasaile chiṭo niskancha
Do you like tea with milk or plain black tea?
दुध भएको चिया कि कालो चिया मन पर्छ?
dukha bhaeko ciyā ki kālo ciyā mana parcha
The children like the new puppy more than the old cat.
बच्चाहरूलाई पुरानो भन्दा नयाँ पिल्लु मन पर्छ।
baccāharūlāī purāno bhanda nayā~ pillu mana parcha
I used to like that song when I was in school.
स्कुल हुँदा त्यो गीत मलाई मन पर्थ्यो।
skula hu~dā tyo gīta malāī mana parthyo
She will like this gift because you chose it with care.
सोचेर छानेकाले यो उपहार उनलाई मन पर्ला।
socera chānekāle yo upahāra unalāī mana paralā
We do not like people who break promises for small gains.
सानो फाइदाका लागि वाचा तोड्नेलाई हामी मन पराउँदैनौं।
sāno phāidākā lāgi vācā toḍnelāī hāmī mana parāu~dainauṃ
He likes reading newspapers more than scrolling on his phone.
फोनमा स्क्रल गर्नुभन्दा उसलाई पत्रिका पढ्न मन पर्छ।
phōnamā skrala garnubhandā usalāī patrikā paḍhna mana parcha
I like your honesty even when your words are hard to hear.
कडा लागे पनि तपाईंको इमानदारी मलाई मन पर्छ।
kaḍā lāge pani tapāīṃko imāndārī malāī mana parcha
They like sitting on the roof and watching the stars at night.
राति छानामा बसेर तारा हेर्न उनीहरूलाई मन पर्छ।
rāti chānāmā basera tārā herna unīharūlāī mana parcha
She does not like cold drinks when she has a sore throat.
घाँटी दुख्दा उनलाई चिसो पेय मन पर्दैन।
ghā~ṭī dukhdā unalāī ciso peya mana pardaina
We would like to invite you to dinner on Saturday evening.
शनिबार साँझ खानामा निमन्त्रणा दिन चाहन्छौं।
śanibāra sā~jha khānamā nimantraṇā dina cāhanchauṃ
I like how you explain difficult ideas in simple words.
कठिन कुरा सजिलो शब्दमा बुझाउनुहुन्छ, त्यो मन पर्छ।
kaṭhina kurā sajilo śabdama bujhāunuhuncha, tyo mana parcha

title: Sentences using "would" in Nepali
content: Would = conditional or polite. Often same as past or conditional verb.
intro: I would go = म जान्थें (conditional). Would you like? = के तपाईं ... चाहनुहुन्छ?

blocks.heading: Would
rows: I would help you tomorrow if I did not have to travel.
भोलि यात्रा नपर्ने भए म तपाईंलाई सहयोग गर्थें।
bholi yātrā naparne bhae ma tapāīṃlāī sahayoga gar-theṃ
She would sing every evening if we had a harmonium at home.
घरमा हार्मोनियम भए हरेक साँझ गाउँथिन्।
gharamā hārmoniyama bhae hareka sā~jha gāu~thin
He would never agree if you spoke to him in that rude tone.
त्यो असभ्य स्वरमा बोल्नुभयो भने उ कहिल्यै मान्ने थिएन।
tyo asabhya svaramā bolnubhayo bhane u kahilyai mānne thiena
We would visit the village every Dashain if the road were safer.
बाटो सुरक्षित भए दशैँमा गाउँ जान्थ्यौं।
bāṭo surakṣita bhae daśai~mā gāu~ jānthyauṃ
They would wait for hours if the train was usually on time.
रेल समयमै आउने भए घण्टौं पर्खिन्थे।
rel samayamai āune bhae ghaṇṭauṃ parkhinthe
I would buy a bicycle if I could save enough money this year.
यो वर्ष पैसा जम्मा भए साइकल किन्थें।
yo varṣa paisā jamā bhae sāikala kintheṃ
You would feel better if you drank more water and slept early.
बढी पानी पिउनुहोस् र छिटो सुत्नुहोस्, अनि सजिलो हुन्थ्यो।
baḍhī pānī piunuhos ra chiṭo sutnuhos, ani sajilo hunthyo
She would translate the letter if her English were stronger.
अङ्ग्रेजी बलियो भए उ पत्र अनुवाद गर्थिन्।
aṅgrejī baliyo bhae u patra anuvāda garthin
We would plant more trees if the municipality gave us saplings.
नगरपालिकाले बिरुवा दिए थप रुख रोप्थ्यौं।
nagarapālikāle biruvā die thapa rukha ropthyauṃ
He would not sign the paper if he did not trust the manager.
प्रबन्धकमा विश्वास नभए उ कागजमा दस्तखत गर्दैनथ्यो।
prabandhakamā viśvāsa nabhae u kāgajamā dastakhata gardainathyo
I would call you first if I heard any news about the result.
नतिजाको खबर पाए पहिले तपाईंलाई फोन गर्थें।
natijāko khabara pāe pahile tapāīṃlāī phona gar-theṃ
They would stay longer if the hotel room were cleaner.
कोठा सफा भए उनीहरू बढी बस्ने थिए।
koṭhā saphā bhae unīharū baḍhī basne thie
You would understand faster if you read the examples aloud.
उदाहरण जोरले पढ्नुभयो भने छिटो बुझ्नुहुन्थ्यो।
udāharaṇa jorle paḍhnubhayo bhane chiṭo bujhnuhunthyo
She would knit sweaters for every child if she had more wool.
अझै उन भए हरेक बच्चालाई स्वेटर बुन्थिन्।
ajhai una bhae hareka baccālāī sveṭara bunthin
We would take the train if the bus strike continued another day.
बस हडताल अर्को दिन पनि भए रेल लिन्थ्यौं।
basa haḍatāla arko dina pani bhae rela linthyauṃ

title: "To Understand" & "To come to know" in Nepali
content: To understand = बुझब. To come to know = जानब, पता लागब.
intro: I understand = म बुझ्छु. I came to know = मलाई थाहा भयो.

blocks.heading: Understand / Come to know
rows: I understand your worry but the doctor said the report is normal.
तपाईंको चिन्ता बुझ्छु तर डाक्टरले प्रतिवेदन सामान्य भन्नुभयो।
tapāīṃko cintā bujh-chu tara ḍākṭarale prativedana sāmānya bhannubhayo
She finally understood the rule after you explained it twice.
दुई पटक बुझाएपछि उनलाई नियम थाहा भयो।
duī paṭaka bujhāepachi unalāī niyama thāhā bhayo
We did not understand his accent at first, then it became easier.
सुरुमा उसको उच्चारण बुझेनौं, पछि सजिलो भयो।
surumā usako uccāraṇa bujhenauṃ, pachi sajilo bhayo
Do you understand that this ticket cannot be refunded?
यो टिकट फिर्ता हुँदैन भन्ने बुझ्नुभयो?
yo ṭikaṭa phirtā hu~dain bhannē bujhnubhayo
He understood the joke late and then laughed loudly.
उसलाई जोक ढिलो बुझ्यो, अनि जोरले हाँस्यो।
usalāī joka ḍhilo bujhyo, ani jorle hā~syo
I came to know the truth only after reading her diary.
उनको डायरी पढेपछि मात्र सत्य थाहा पाएँ।
unako ḍāyarī paḍhepachi mātra satya thāhā pāe~
She came to know about the party through a message on her phone.
फोनको सन्देशबाट पार्टीको खबर थाहा पाइन्।
phona sandeśabāṭa pārṭīko khabara thāhā pāin
We came to know that the shop would close for a week.
पसल एक हप्ता बन्द हुन्छ भन्ने थाहा पायौं।
pasala eka haptā banda huncha bhannē thāhā pāyauṃ
They came to know the route by asking people at every crossing.
हरेक चोकमा सोध्दै बाटो थाहा पाए।
hareka cokamā sodhdai bāṭo thāhā pāe
I understand Nepali better now than when I first arrived.
पहिलो दिनभन्दा अहिले नेपाली बढी बुझ्छु।
pahilo dinabhanda ahile nepālī baḍhī bujh-chu
Please speak slowly so that everyone can understand clearly.
सबैले स्पष्ट बुझुन् भनेर ढिलो बोल्नुहोस्।
sabaile spaṣṭa bujun bhannera ḍhilo bolnuhos
He did not understand why she left without saying goodbye.
बिदाइ नभनी किन गइन् भन्ने उसले बुझेन।
bidāi nabanī kina gain bhannē usale bujhena
I will understand if you cancel the plan because of rain.
पानीले योजना रद्द गर्नुभयो भने बुझ्छु।
pānīle yojanā radda garnubhayo bhane bujh-chu
She understands mathematics faster than most students in her class.
कक्षाका धेरै विद्यार्थीभन्दा उ अङ्कित छिटो बुझ्छिन्।
kakṣākā dherai vidyārthībhandā u aṅkita chiṭo buj-chin
We came to know each other while volunteering at the school fair.
विद्यालय मेलामा सेवा गर्दा हामी एकअर्कालाई चिन्यौं।
vidyālaya melāmā sevā gardā hāmī ekarkālāī cin-yauṃ

title: Add a question tag in Nepali
content: Question tags: की? (isn't it?), नहि? (right?).
intro: You are coming, right? = तपाईं आउँदै हुनुहुन्छ, हैन? It is good, no? = राम्रो छ, हैन?

blocks.heading: Question tags
rows: You are coming tomorrow, aren't you?
He has already eaten, hasn't he?
We can sit here, can't we?
She knows the answer, doesn't she?
It is cold today, isn't it?
You will tell me later, won't you?
I should leave now, shouldn't I?
We met last year, didn't we?
You have finished the work, haven't you?
He did not call, did he?
Let us go together, shall we?

title: Using verb "To remember" in Nepali
content: To remember = याद गर्नु, सम्झनु. I remember = मलाई याद छ.
intro: Remember = याद. I don't remember = मलाई याद छैन.

blocks.heading: To remember
rows: I remember your grandfather's kind smile from my childhood.
Do you remember where we parked the scooter last night?
हिजो रात स्कुटर कहाँ राखेका थियौं सम्झनुहुन्छ?
hijo rāta skuṭara kahā~ rākhekā thiyauṃ sam̃jhnuhuncha
She suddenly remembered that she had left the keys in the shop.
उनलाई एकाएक सम्झियो साँचो पसलमै छोडेकी।
unalāī ekāeka sam̃hiyo sā~co pasalamai choḍekī
We will always remember the help you gave during the flood.
He cannot remember the password he set yesterday.
I try to remember ten new words every single day.
हरेक दिन दस नयाँ शब्द सम्झन प्रयास गर्छु।
hareka dina dasa nayā~ śabda sam̃jhana prayāsa gar-chu
You should remember to switch off the fan when you leave.
She remembered the recipe though she had not cooked it for years.
वर्षौं नपकाए पनि विधि उनलाई सम्झियो।
varṣauṃ napakāe pani vidhi unalāī sam̃hiyo
We remembered to carry umbrellas when we saw the dark clouds.
बादल कालो देख्दा छाता बोक्न सम्झियौं।
bādala kālo dekh-dā chātā bokna sam̃hiyauṃ
He will remember this lesson if you explain it with a story.
कथा सुनाएर बुझाउनुभयो भने यो पाठ सम्झिनेछ।
kathā sunāera bujhāunubhayo bhane yo pāṭha sam̃jhinecha
I do not remember his name but I remember his blue jacket.
नाम सम्झिनँ तर नीलो ज्याकेट सम्झन्छु।
nāma sam̃jinnā tara nīlo jyākeṭa sam̃jhanchu
They remembered to wish her on her birthday at midnight.
जन्मदिनको बेलामा बधाई दिन राति नै सम्झिए।
janmadinako belāmā badhāī dina rāti nai sam̃jhie
Please remember to lock the gate when you return late.
ढिलो फर्कँदा गेट बन्द गर्न नबिर्सनुहोला।
ḍhilo pharka~dā geṭa banda garna nabirsanuholā
She remembers every student who failed and came back stronger.
फेल भएर फर्केका विद्यार्थी उनी सम्झिन्छिन्।
phela bhaera pharkekā vidyārthī unī sam̃jhanchin
We should remember that small mistakes can cause big accidents.
सानो गल्तीले ठूलो दुर्घटना हुन सक्छ भन्ने सम्झनुपर्छ।
sāno galtīle ṭhūlo durghaṭanā huna sakcha bhannē sam̃jhnuparcha

title: Using "To Want"/"To Need" with other verbs in Nepali
content: Want + verb: चाहनु + infinitive. म खान चाहन्छु (I want to eat).
intro: I want to go = म जान चाहन्छु. She needs to rest = उनलाई आराम चाहिन्छ.

blocks.heading: Want/Need with verbs
rows: I want to learn Nepali well enough to read newspapers.
पत्रिका पढ्न सकूँ भन्ने नेपाली राम्रोसँग सिक्न चाहन्छु।
patrikā paḍhna sakū~ bhannē nepālī rāmrosa~ga sikna cāhanchu
She wants to sleep early but she has to finish this report.
छिटो सुत्न चाहन्छिन् तर प्रतिवेदन सकाउनु पर्छ।
chiṭo sutna cāhanchin tara prativedana sakāunu parcha
We want to visit Lumbini next month if tickets are cheap.
अर्को महिना सस्तो टिकट भए लुम्बिनी जान चाहन्छौं।
arko mahinā sasto ṭikaṭa bhae lumbinī jāna cāhanchauṃ
He wants to drink water before he takes any medicine.
You want to leave now but the rain has become heavier.
अब जान चाहनुहुन्छ तर पानी बढेको छ।
aba jāna cāhanuhuncha tara pānī baḍheko cha
They want to hire a tutor who can teach maths simply.
गणित सजिलै पढाउने शिक्षक राख्न चाहन्छन्।
gaṇita sajilai paḍhāune śikṣaka rākhna cāhanchan
I wanted to call you but the network was dead all evening.
फोन गर्न चाहेको थिएँ तर साँझभरि नेटवर्क थिएन।
phona garna cāheko thie~ tara sā~jhabhari neṭavaraka thiena
She needs to rest because she has been coughing all night.
We need to buy rice before the shop closes at eight.
आठ बजे बन्द हुँदैछ, चामल किन्नु पर्छ।
āṭha baje banda hu~daicha, cāmala kinnu parcha
He wants to save money to repair the roof before monsoon.
मनसुनअघि छाना मर्मत गर्न पैसा जम्मा गर्न चाहन्छ।
manasunaghi chānā marmata garna paisā jamā garna cāhancha
I want to understand this poem without using a dictionary.
शब्दकोश नचलाई यो कविता बुझ्न चाहन्छु।
śabdakośa nacalāī yo kavitā bujhna cāhanchu
You need to sign here if you agree with these conditions.
यी सर्त मान्नुहुन्छ भने यहाँ दस्तखत गर्नु पर्छ।
ī sarta mānnuhuncha bhane yahā~ dastakhata garnu parcha
They want to move to a quieter flat away from the main road.
मुख्य सडकबाट टाढा शान्त फ्ल्याटमा सर्न चाहन्छन्।
mukhya saḍakabāṭa ṭāḍhā śānta phlyāṭamā sarna cāhanchan
She wants to invite her teacher to the graduation ceremony.
We need to leave at dawn if we want to avoid traffic.

title: Using "To Want" with other person in Nepali
content: Want someone to do: ... लाई ... गर्न लगाउनु / चाहनु.
intro: I want you to come = म चाहन्छु तिमी आऊ / तपाईं आउनुहोस्.

blocks.heading: Want with person
rows: My mother wants me to become a doctor, but I like engineering.
आमाले डाक्टर बन भन्नुहुन्छ, तर मलाई इन्जिनियरिङ मन पर्छ।
āmāle ḍākṭara bana bhannuhuncha, tara malāī injiniyariṅa mana parcha
His boss wants him to work on Sunday without extra pay.
मालिकले आइतबार काम गर्न भन्छ तर थप तलब छैन।
mālikale āitabāra kāma garna bhancha tara thapa talaba chaina
She wants her daughter to study science in the capital.
We want our son to learn both Nepali and English at school.
छोराले स्कुलमा नेपाली र अङ्ग्रेजी दुवै सिकोस् भन्छौं।
chorāle skulamā nepālī ra aṅgrejī duvai sikos bhanchauṃ
They want the tenant to pay rent before the fifth of every month.
भाडा हरेक महिना पाँच गते अघि तिरोस् भन्छन्।
bhāḍā hareka mahinā pā̃ca gate aghi tiras bhanchan
I want you to be honest with me about the exam result.
He wants his friend to drive slowly on the mountain road.
साथीले पहाडको बाटोमा ढिलो गाडी चलाओस् भन्छ।
sāthīle pahāḍako bāṭomā ḍhilo gāḍī calāos bhancha
She does not want anyone to touch her laptop without asking.
We want the mechanic to replace the brake pads today.
मेकानिकले आज नै ब्रेक प्याड बदलोस् भन्छौं।
mekānikale āja nai breka pyāḍa badalos bhanchauṃ
The teacher wants every student to submit homework on Monday.
सोमबार सबैले गृहकार्य बुझाउनु पर्छ भन्नुहुन्छ।
somabāra sabaile gr̥hakārya bujhāunu parcha bhannuhuncha
I want my brother to rest because he looks very tired.
भाइ थाकेको देख्छु, आराम गरोस् भन्छु।
bhāi thākeko dekh-chu, ārāma garos bhanchu
Parents want their children to respect elders and teachers.
बच्चाले जेठो र शिक्षकको सम्मान गरून् भन्छन्।
baccāle jeṭho ra śikṣakako sammān garūn bhanchan
She wants him to call her as soon as he reaches Kathmandu.
We do not want guests to feel uncomfortable in our small flat.
सानो फ्ल्याटमा असुविधा नहोस् भन्ने चाहन्छौं।
sāno phlyāṭamā asuvidhā nahos bhannē cāhanchauṃ
He wants the company to pay overtime for last month's work.
गत महिनाको कामका लागि ओभरटाइम दिनुपर्छ भन्छ।
gata mahīnākō kāmakā lāgi obharataima dinuparcha bhancha

title: Using phrase "supposed to" in Nepali
content: Supposed to = करबाक चाही, होबाक चाही. Expected or intended.
intro: I was supposed to go = म जानुपर्थ्यो. You are supposed to come = तपाईं आउनुपर्छ.

blocks.heading: Supposed to
rows: You are supposed to wear formal clothes for the interview.
अन्तर्वार्ताका लागि औपचारिक लुगा लगाउनु पर्छ।
antarvārtākā lāgi aupacārika lugā lagāunu parcha
We are supposed to arrive ten minutes before the programme starts.
कार्यक्रम सुरु हुनुअघि दस मिनेट अघि पुग्नु पर्छ।
kāryakrama suru hunuaghi dasa mineṭa aghi pugnu parcha
He is supposed to return the book by Friday without fail.
She is not supposed to eat cold food while she has a fever.
ज्वरो छँदा चिसो खाना खानु हुँदैन।
jvaro cha~dā ciso khānā khānu hu~dain
I am supposed to call the landlord before I paint the walls.
भित्ता रङ्ग लगाउनु अघि मालिकलाई फोन गर्नु पर्छ।
bhittā raṅga lagāunu agi mālikalāī phona garnu parcha
They are supposed to clean the stairs once a week.
हरेक हप्ता पखेटा सफा गर्नु पर्छ।
hareka haptā pakheṭā saphā garnu parcha
You were supposed to bring the file yesterday afternoon.
We were supposed to meet at six but the bus was late.
छ बजे भेट्नु पर्थ्यो तर बस ढिलो भयो।
cha baje bheṭnu parthyo tara basa ḍhilo bhayo
He is supposed to know the rules because he signed the contract.
सम्झौतामा दस्तखत गरेकाले नियम थाहा हुनुपर्छ।
sam̃jhautāmā dastakhata garekāle niyama thāhā hunuparcha
The medicine is supposed to work within thirty minutes.
औषधि तीस मिनेटभित्र असर देखाउनु पर्छ।
auṣadhi tīsa mineṭabhitra asara dekhāunu parcha
I am supposed to pick up my niece from school at four.
चार बजे स्कुलबाट भतिजीलाई लिन जानु पर्छ।
cāra baje skulabāṭa bhatijīlāī lina jānu parcha
You are supposed to keep your voice down in the library.
We are supposed to submit tax papers before the last date.
अन्तिम मितिअघि करका कागज बुझाउनु पर्छ।
antima mitiaghi karakā kāgaja bujhāunu parcha
She is supposed to chair the meeting if the manager is absent.
प्रबन्धक अनुपस्थित भए बैठक उनी सञ्चालन गर्नु पर्छ।
prabandhaka anupasthita bhae baiṭhaka unī sañcālana garnu parcha
They are supposed to wear helmets; the rule is for everyone.
हेलमेट लगाउनु पर्छ; नियम सबैका लागि हो।
helameṭa lagāunu parcha; niyama sabaikā lāgi ho

title: Using "To Like" with other verbs in Nepali
content: Like + verb: खान मन लाग्छ, पढ्न मन लाग्छ.
intro: I like to eat = मलाई खान मन लाग्छ. She likes to read = उनलाई पढ्न मन लाग्छ.

blocks.heading: Like with verbs
rows: I like to drink hot tea while I read the morning news.
बिहानको समाचार पढ्दा तातो चिया पिउन मन पर्छ।
bihānako samācāra paḍhdā tāto ciyā piuna mana parcha
She likes to walk by the river when the weather is cool.
मौसम चिसो हुँदा नदी किनार हिँड्न मन पर्छ।
mausama ciso hu~dā nadī kināra hiḍna mana parcha
We like to cook together on holidays and try new recipes.
छुट्टीमा सँगै खाना पकाउन र नयाँ विधि प्रयोग गर्न मन लाग्छ।
chuṭṭīmā saṃgai khānā pakāuna ra nayā~ vidhi prayog garna mana lāgcha
He does not like to wake early but his job forces him to.
उ बिहान छिटो उठ्न मन पराउँदैन तर जागिरले बाध्य पार्छ।
u bihāna chiṭo uṭhna mana parāu~dain tara jāgirale bādhya pārcha
They like to watch old films on rainy evenings at home.
पानी पर्दा घरमै पुरानो चलचित्र हेर्न मन पर्छ।
pānī pardā gharamai purāno calacitra herna mana parcha
I would like to learn to play the madal if I find a teacher.
शिक्षक पाए मादल बजाउन सिक्न मन छ।
śikṣaka pāe mādala bajāuna sikna mana cha
You like to argue for fun but sometimes it hurts feelings.
मजाकका लागि बहस गर्नुहुन्छ तर कहिलेकाहीँ मन दुख्छ।
majākakā lāgi bahasa garnuhuncha tara kahilekāhī~ mana duk-cha
She likes to paint flowers more than she likes to paint faces.
अनुहारभन्दा फूल कोर्न उनलाई बढी मन पर्छ।
anuhārabhandā phūla korna unalāī baḍhī mana parcha
We do not like to waste food when so many people go hungry.
धेरै भोकाएका बेला खाना बर्बाद गर्न मन पर्दैन।
dherai bhokāekā belā khānā barabāda garna mana pardaina
He likes to fix broken radios though he is not a professional.
पेशेवर नभए पनि बिग्रिएको रेडियो मर्मत गर्न मन पर्छ।
peśevara nabhae pani bigrieko reḍiyo marmata garna mana parcha
I like to listen to folk songs while I wash the dishes.
भाँडा माझ्दा लोक गीत सुन्न मन पर्छ।
bhā~ḍā mājh-dā loka gīta sunna mana parcha
They like to travel by train because they can see the scenery.
दृश्य हेर्न रेलमा यात्रा गर्न मन पर्छ।
dr̥śya herna relamā yātrā garna mana parcha
She likes to write poems but she rarely shows them to anyone.
कविता लेख्न मन पर्छ तर कमैले देखाउँछिन्।
kavitā lekhna mana parcha tara kamaile dekhāu~chin
We like to sit on the porch and watch people pass by.
गल्लीमा मान्छे हेर्न खापमा बस्न मन लाग्छ।
gallīmā mānche herna khāpamā basna mana lāgcha
I like to help you cook when you try a new dish.
नयाँ परिकार बनाउँदा सहयोग गर्न मन पर्छ।
nayā~ parikāra banāu~dā sahayoga garna mana parcha

title: Verbs which use preposition "To" with subject and verb form as per object in Nepali
content: Verbs where subject takes case and verb agrees with object. देखब (see), सुनब (hear).
intro: I see you = म तिमीलाई देख्छु. He gave me = उसले मलाई दियो.

blocks.heading: Verbs with To (object)
rows: I believe in your honesty even when the situation is difficult.
अवस्था गाह्रो भए पनि तपाईंको इमानदारीमा विश्वास गर्छु।
avasthā gāhro bhae pani tapāīṃko imāndārīmā viśvāsa gar-chu
She listens to her grandmother's stories with full attention.
We depend on this small pump for water during the dry season.
सुक्खा मौसममा पानीका लागि यो सानो पम्पमा भर पर्छौं।
sukkhā mausamamā pānīkā lāgi yo sāno pampamā bhara parchauṃ
He apologised to the teacher for arriving late to class.
कक्षामा ढिलो भएकाले शिक्षकसँग माफी मागे।
kakṣāmā ḍhilo bhaekāle śikṣakasa~ga māphī māge
They laughed at the joke but nobody felt insulted.
जोकमा सबै हाँसे तर कसैलाई बेइज्जत लागेन।
jokamā sabai hā~se tara kasailāī beijjata lāgena
I agree with your plan to plant trees along the canal.
नहर किनार रुख रोप्ने योजनामा सहमत छु।
nahara kināra rukha ropne yojanāmā sahamata chu
She shouted at the dog when it ran towards the baby.
We trust the new coach because he explains every drill clearly.
नयाँ प्रशिक्षकले हरेक अभ्यास स्पष्ट बुझाउँछन्।
nayā~ praśikṣakale hareka abhyāsa spaṣṭa bujhāu~chan
He dreams of building a small library in his village one day.
एक दिन गाउँमा सानो पुस्तकालय बनाउने सपना देख्छ।
eka dina gāu~mā sāno pustakālaya banāune sapanā dekh-cha
I replied to his email as soon as I read it this morning.
बिहान पढ्नेबित्तिकै उसको इमेलको जवाफ दिएँ।
bihāna paḍhnebittikai usako imelako javāpha die~
She belongs to a family of musicians from eastern Nepal.
उ पूर्वी नेपालका संगीतकार परिवारकी हुन्।
u pūrvī nepālakā saṅgītakāra parivārakī hun
We objected to the noisy construction work after midnight.
He pretended not to hear when they asked him for money.
पैसा माग्दा उसले सुनेन भनी नाटक गर्यो।
paisā māgdā usale sunena bhanī nāṭaka garyo
I thank you from my heart for standing by me in trouble.
They prefer tea to coffee on cold winter mornings.

title: Short forms in spoken Nepali
content: Colloquial shortenings vary by region; standard written Nepali uses छ, छु, छैन.
intro: Spoken forms may drop syllables; learn the standard school form first.

blocks.heading: Spoken short forms
rows: I will come a bit later — traffic is bad on the ring road.
अलि ढिलो आउँछु — रिङ्गरोडमा जाम छ।
ali ḍhilo āu~chu — riṅgaroḍamā jāma cha
Don't worry, I'll do it — I have free time this evening.
नडराउनु, म गर्छु — साँझ फुर्सद छ।
naḍarāunu, ma gar-chu — sā~jha phursada cha
Yeah, that's fine — we can meet at the usual tea shop.
No problem — I'll send you the photo on WhatsApp.
Really? — I thought you were still in Pokhara.
साँच्चै? — पोखरामै हुनुहुन्छ भन्ने लागेको थिएन।
sā~cchai? — pokharāmai hunuhuncha bhannē lāgeko thiena
Come on — the bus is about to leave without us.
See you — I'll call when I reach home.
Got it — I'll bring milk on my way back.
Whatever — I don't want to argue about it now.
जे होस् — अहिले यसमा बहस गर्न मन छैन।
je hos — ahile yasamā bahasa garna mana chaina
Same here — I also felt tired after that long walk.
Hang on — I'm just locking the door.
Not yet — the rice is still cooking.
अझै होइन — भात अझै पाकिरहेको छ।
ajhai hoina — bhāt ajhai pāki-raheko cha
Maybe — depends on whether it rains tomorrow.
हुन सक्छ — भोलि पानी पर्यो भने।
huna sakcha — bholi pānī paryo bhane
Exactly — that's what I wanted to say.
ठ्याक्कै — मैले पनि त्यही भन्न खोजेको।
ṭhyākkai — maile pani tyahī bhanna khojeko
Never mind — we can try again next week.
छोड्नुस् — अर्को हप्ता फेरि प्रयास गर्छौं।
choḍnus — arko haptā pheri prayāsa gar-chauṃ

title: Greeting, Wishes, Blessings, Slogans in Nepali
content: Greetings and good wishes in Nepali.
intro: प्रणाम, स्वागत, धन्यवाद, शुभकामना, बधाई.

blocks.heading: Greetings and wishes
rows: When elders visit our home we greet them with a respectful namaskar.
जेठा मान्छे घर आउँदा हामी नमस्कार गर्छौं।
jeṭhā mānche ghara āu~dā hāmī namaskāra gar-chauṃ
Welcome — please come in and sit wherever you feel comfortable.
स्वागत छ — भित्र पस्नुहोस्, जहाँ सजिलो लाग्छ बस्नुहोस्।
svāgata cha — bhitra pasnuhos, jahā~ sajilo lāgcha basnuhos
Thank you for bringing fresh vegetables on such a rainy day.
यस्तो पानी परेको दिन तरकारी ल्याउनुभएकोमा धन्यवाद।
yasto pānī pareko dina tarkārī lyāunubhaekomā dhanyavāda
Good morning — I hope you slept well after the long journey.
