शुभ प्रभात — लामो यात्रापछि राम्रोसँग सुत्नुभयो होला।
śubha prabhāta — lāmo yātrāpachi rāmrosa~ga sutnubhayo holā
Good evening — the tea is ready if you would like a cup.
शुभ साँझ — चिया तयार छ, चाहनुहुन्छ भने लिनुहोस्।
śubha sā~jha — ciyā tayāra cha, cāhanuhuncha bhane linuhos
Good night — lock the gate and sleep peacefully.
शुभ रात्रि — गेट बन्द गरेर शान्त सुत्नुहोस्।
śubha rātri — geṭa banda garera śānta sutnuhos
Happy birthday — may this year bring you health and peace.
जन्मदिनको शुभकामना — यो वर्ष स्वास्थ्य र शान्ति होस्।
janmadinako śubhakāmanā — yo varṣa svāsthya ra śānti hos
Congratulations on your new job; your family must be proud.
नयाँ जागिरमा बधाई; परिवारलाई गर्व छ होला।
nayā~ jāgiramā badhāī; parivāralāī garva cha holā
Good luck with the exam — you have studied very hard.
Get well soon — we are all praying for your quick recovery.
छिट्टै निको हुनुहोस् — सबै छिटो निको होस् भन्दैछौं।
chiṭṭai niko hunuhos — sabai chiṭo niko hos bandaichauṃ
Happy New Year — may your plans succeed step by step.
नयाँ वर्षको शुभकामना — योजना क्रमशः सफल होस्।
nayā~ varṣako śubhakāmanā — yojanā kramaśaḥ saphala hos
Happy Tihar — may your home stay bright with lamps and laughter.
शुभ दीपावली — घर उज्यालो र हाँसोले भरियोस्।
śubha dīpāvalī — ghara ujyālo ra hā~sole bhariyos
May God bless you and your children with long life.
ईश्वरले तपाईं र बच्चाहरूलाई दीर्घायु दिनुहोस्।
īśvarale tapāīṃ ra baccāharūlāī dīrghāyu dinuhos
Have a nice day — take care on the crowded roads.
दिन शुभ रहोस् — भीडमा सावधान हुनुहोला।
dina śubha rahos — bhīḍamā sāvadhāna hunuholā
Bon voyage — message me as soon as you land safely.
शुभ यात्रा — सुरक्षित उत्रिएपछि फोन गर्नुहोस्।
śubha yātrā — surakṣita utriepachi phona garnuhos
Cheers to your health — drink water between each cup of tea.
स्वास्थ्यको जय होस् — प्रत्येक चियापछि पानी पिउनुहोस्।
svāsthryako jaya hos — pratyeka ciyāpachi pānī piunuhos
Enjoy your meal — the rice is still hot from the pot.
राम्रो खाना खानुहोस् — भात भट्टीबाटै तातो छ।
rāmro khānā khānuhos — bhāt bhaṭṭībāṭai tāto cha
Season's greetings to you and everyone in your office.
तपाईं र कार्यालयका सबैलाई चाडपर्वको शुभकामना।
tapāīṃ ra kāryālayakā sabailāī cāḍaparvako śubhakāmanā
Merry Christmas — may this holiday bring peace to your street.
क्रिसमसको शुभकामना — यो चाडले गल्लीमा शान्ति होस्।
krisamasako śubhakāmanā — yo cāḍale gallīmā śānti hos
Well done — the whole team is proud of your honesty.
राम्रो भयो — टिमले तपाईंको इमानदारीको गर्व गर्छ।
rāmro bhayo — ṭimale tapāīṃko imāndārīko garva gar-cha
Best wishes for your wedding — may you respect each other always.
विवाहको हार्दिक शुभकामना — सधैं एकअर्काको सम्मान गर्नुहोला।
vivāhako hārdika śubhakāmanā — sadhaiṃ ekarkākō sammān garnuholā
Have a safe journey — the mountain road is slippery after rain.
शुभ यात्रा — पानीपछि पहाडको बाटो चिप्लो छ।
śubha yātrā — pānīpachi pahāḍako bāṭo ciplo cha
May you succeed in every exam you take this year.
यो वर्ष दिने परीक्षामा सबैमा सफल होस्।
yo varṣa dine parīkṣāmā sabaimā saphala hos
With warm regards from our whole family to yours.
हाम्रो परिवारबाट तपाईंको परिवारलाई नम्र सम्मान सहित।
hāmro parivārabāṭa tapāīṃko parivāralāī namra sammāna sahita
Farewell for now — we will wait for your next letter.
अहिलेलाई बिदाइ — अर्को पत्रको पर्खाइमा छौं।
ahilelāī bidāi — arko patrako parkhāimā chauṃ
Take care of yourself in this cold weather.
यो चिसो मौसममा आफ्नो ख्याल गर्नुहोला।
yo ciso mausamamā āphno khyāla garnuholā
See you soon — bring your children when you visit next time.
छिट्टै भेटौँला — अर्को पटक बच्चाहरू लिएर आउनुहोला।
chiṭṭai bheṭau~lā — arko paṭaka baccāharū liera āunuholā
Pleased to meet you — I have heard a lot about your work.
भेटेर खुसी लाग्यो — तपाईंको कामबारे धेरै सुनेको थिएँ।
bheṭera khusī lāgyo — tapāīṃko kāmabāre dherai suneko thie~
If someone asks how you are, you can say you are fine.
कस्तो छ भनेर सोधे राम्रो छ भन्न सक्नुहुन्छ।
kasto cha bhanera sodhe rāmro cha bhanna saknuhuncha

title: Compound verbs / Phrases in Nepali
content: Compound verbs: main verb + auxiliary (लाग, दे, ले, पड़). खा लेल, दे देल.
intro: Complete action: खा लेल (ate up), दे देल (gave away), आबि गेल (came).

blocks.heading: Compound verbs
rows: I ate up every grain of rice because the dal was so tasty.
दाल धेरै मिठो भएकाले भात सबै सकें।
dāla dherai miṭho bhaekāle bhātā sabai sake~
She drank up the juice before anyone else could taste it.
अरूले चाख्नुअघि नै उनले जुस सकाइन्।
arūle cākhnuaghi nai unile jusa sakāin
We gave away old clothes to the flood victims last week.
गत हप्ता पुरानो लुगा बाढीपीडितलाई दिइयो।
gata haptā purāno lugā bāḍhīpīḍitalāī diiyo
He read through the whole report in one long sitting.
उसले एकै बसाइमा पूरा प्रतिवेदन पढ्यो।
usale ekai basāimā pūrā prativedana paḍhyo
They brought the heavy bags upstairs without complaining.
गुनासो नगरी भारी झोला माथि ल्याए।
gunāso nagarī bhārī jholā māthi lyāe
I threw away the rotten fruit so the children would not eat it.
बच्चाले नखाऊन् भनेर बिग्रिएको फल फ्याँकें।
baccāle nakhāun bhannera bigrieko phala phyā~ke~
She locked up the shop and walked home under the street lights.
पसल बन्द गरेर बत्तीमुनि हिँडेर घर गइन्।
pasala banda garera battīmuni hiḍera ghara gain
We sat down on the bench and watched the river flow quietly.
बेन्चमा बसेर नदी शान्त बगेको हेर्यौं।
bemcamā basera nadī śānta bageko heryauṃ
He woke up at dawn and boiled milk for the whole family.
बिहानै उठेर परिवारका लागि दुध उमाल्यो।
bihānai uṭhera parivārakā lāgi dudha umālyo
I wrote down the phone number before I forgot it again.
फेरि बिर्सनु अघि नम्बर कागजमा लेखें।
pheri birsanu aghi nambara kāgajamā lekhe~
They cut up the vegetables while we heated the oil.
हामी तेल तताउँदा उनीहरूले तरकारी काटे।
hāmī tela tatāu~dā unīharūle tarkārī kāṭe
She washed off the mud from her shoes at the tap outside.
We paid off the small loan before the interest grew higher.
He looked up the word in the dictionary and smiled.
I picked up the crying child and carried her to her mother.

title: Calling or Addressing someone in Nepali
content: Ways to address: ओहो, अरे, हे, कानि सुनू (excuse me).
intro: Hey! = ओहो, अरे. Sir = महाशय. Madam = महोदया.

blocks.heading: Addressing people
rows: We call our grandfather Hajur Baba with great affection.
Young people often say dai to a slightly older male friend.
अलि जेठो साथीलाई धेरैले दाइ भन्छन्।
ali jeṭho sāthīlāī dheraile dāi bhanchan
She politely addressed the officer as sir while handing the form.
Children in our lane call the kind shopkeeper uncle.
Do not use rude words when you speak to your mother-in-law.
सासुलाई असभ्य शब्दले सम्बोधन गर्न हुँदैन।
sāsulāī asabhya śabdale sambodhana garnu hu~dain
The teacher asked everyone to call her didi instead of madam.
शिक्षकले म्याडम नभनी दिदी भन्न भन्नुभयो।
śikṣakale myāḍama nabhanī didī bhanna bhannubhayo
He introduced me to his friend as his younger brother.
In formal letters we begin with respected sir or madam.
औपचारिक पत्रमा आदरणीय महोदय वा महोदया लेख्छौं।
aupacārika patramā ādarṇīya mahodaya vā mahodayā lekh-chauṃ
The crowd shouted slogans addressing the minister by name.
भीडले मन्त्रीको नाम लिएर नारा लगायो।
bhīḍale mantrīko nāma liera nārā lagāyo
She calls her neighbour sister though they are not related.
नाता नभए पनि छिमेकीलाई दिदी भन्छिन्।
nāta nabhae pani chimekīlāī didī bhanchin
Please do not address strangers with very familiar nicknames.
अजनबीलाई अति घरेलु उपनामले सम्बोधन गर्न हुँदैन।
ajanabīlāī ati gharelu upanāmale sambodhana garnu hu~dain
We respectfully say tapain when we talk to our teacher.
The little boy calls every soldier in uniform uncle.
सानो केटाले युनिफर्म लगाएका सैनिकलाई काका भन्छ।
sāno keṭāle yunipharma lagāekā sainikālāī kākā bhancha
She wrote the envelope using my full name and title.
लिफाफामा मेरो पूरा नाम र पद लेखिन्।
lipāphāmā mero pūrā nāma ra pada lekhin
They greeted the guests by folding hands and saying namaskar.

title: Exclamations in Nepali
content: Exclamations: अहा!, ओह!, हाय!, वाह!, क्या!.
intro: Wow! = वाह! Oh no! = हाय! Alas! = अहा!

blocks.heading: Exclamations
rows: Oh no — the milk boiled over and spilled on the stove!
Wow — look at that rainbow behind the hill!
Ouch — I stubbed my toe on this heavy stone.
Hey — wait for me; I cannot run that fast!
हे — पर्खनुस्, म त्यति छिटो दौड्न सक्दिन!
he — parkhanus, ma tyati chiṭo dauḍna sakdina
Alas — we missed the last bus and it is already dark.
हाय — अन्तिम बस छुट्यो, अँध्यारो भइसक्यो।
hāy — antima basa chuṭyo, a~dhyāro bhaisakyo
Bravo — you finished the race even with a sore knee.
शाबास — घुँडा दुखे पनि दौड सकायौ।
śābās — ghu~ḍā dukhe pani dauḍa sakāyau
Yuck — this milk smells sour; throw it away.
फि — दुध बिग्रिएको गन्ध छ, फ्याँकिदेऊ।
phi — dudha bigrieko gandha cha, phyā~kidieu
Hurrah — our team finally won the school cup!
हुर्रे — हाम्रो टिमले अन्त्यमा स्कुल कप जित्यो!
hurre — hāmro ṭimale antyamā skula kapa jityo
Shh — the baby is sleeping; do not shout.
Damn — I forgot the keys inside the locked room!
Thank goodness — the doctor said the X-ray is normal.
धन्य ईश्वर — एक्सरे सामान्य छ भन्नुभयो।
dhanya īśvara — eksare sāmānya cha bhannubhayo
What a pity — the picnic is cancelled because of the storm.
धेरै दुःख — आँधीले पिकनिक रद्द भयो।
dherai duḥkha — ā~dhīle pikanika radda bhayo
How beautiful — the whole valley is white with snow!
Good heavens — that truck almost hit the bicycle!
हे प्रभु — ट्रकले साइकल जस्तै ठोक्न लाग्यो!
he prabhu — ṭrakale sāikala jastai ṭhokna lāgyo
Phew — I thought we would miss the train.
फ्यु — रेल छुट्ला जस्तो लागेको थियो।
phyu — rela chuṭalā jasto lāgeko thiyo
Surprise — your cousin came from abroad without telling anyone!
आश्चर्य — भिनाजु कसैलाई नभनी विदेशबाट आए!
āścarya — bhināju kasailāī nabanī videśabāṭa āe
Help — someone fell into the canal!
Cheers — here is to your new job and good health!
जय होस् — नयाँ जागिर र स्वास्थ्य!
jaya hos — nayā~ jāgira ra svāsthya
Blast — the power went off right in the middle of the film!
My word — you have grown so tall in two years!
है — दुई वर्षमा कति अग्लो भयौ!
hai — duī varṣamā kati aglo bhayau
For shame — he blamed the child for his own mistake!
Hush — listen — I think someone is at the door.
चुप — सुन — ढोकामा कोही छ जस्तो छ।
cup — suna — ḍhokāmā kohī cha jasto cha
Well done indeed — the whole village is proud of you.
Oh dear — the cake burned while I was on the phone.
अहो — फोनमा कुरा गर्दा केक पोलियो।
aho — phonamā kurā gardā keka poliyo

title: Requesting someone – "May I", "Can you" etc. in Nepali
content: Polite requests: के म ... सक्छु? के तपाईं ... सक्नुहुन्छ?
intro: May I come in? = के म भित्र आउन सक्छु? Can you help? = के तपाईं मद्दत गर्न सक्नुहुन्छ?

blocks.heading: Requests
rows: May I open the window — the kitchen is full of smoke?
झ्याल खोल्न पाऊँ? भान्सामा धुवाँ भरिएको छ।
jhyāla kholna pāū~? bhānsāmā dhuvā~ bharieko cha
Can you pass me the salt without getting up?
Could you speak a little more slowly, please?
Would you mind if I sat next to you on this bench?
Can you tell me which bus goes to the airport?
विमानस्थल जाने कुन बस हो भन्न सक्नुहुन्छ?
vimānasthala jāne kuna basa ho bhanna saknuhuncha
May I borrow your pen for one minute?
एक मिनेटका लागि कलम सापटी लिन पाऊँ?
eka mineṭakā lāgi kalama sāpaṭī lina pāū~
Could you help me carry this bag to the third floor?
यो झोला तेस्रो तलामा पुर्‍याउन मद्दत गर्नुहुन्छ?
yo jholā tesro talāmā puryāuna maddata garnuhuncha
Would you please call a taxi for my grandmother?
Can I pay with my card or do you need cash?
कार्डले तिर्न मिल्छ कि नगद चाहिन्छ?
kārḍale tirna milcha ki nagada cāhincha
May I take this empty chair at your table?
Could you repeat the last sentence, please?
Would you turn down the music a little after ten?
दस पछि संगीत अलि कम गर्नुहुन्छ?
dasa pachi saṅgīta ali kama garnuhuncha
Can you watch my bag while I use the washroom?
म शौचालय जाँदा झोला हेर्न सक्नुहुन्छ?
ma śaucālaya jā~dā jholā herna saknuhuncha
May I ask you one personal question?
Could you send me the address by message tonight?

title: Showing uncertainty using May, Might in Nepali
content: Uncertainty: होयत (might), सकत (may). बहुत सकत (maybe).
intro: It may rain = बरखा होयत. He might come = ओ आबत. Maybe = बहुत सकत.

blocks.heading: May / Might
rows: It may rain tonight, so take an umbrella just in case.
आज रात पानी पर्न सक्छ, सावधानीका लागि छाता लिनुहोस्।
āja rāta pānī parna sakcha, sāvadhānīkā lāgi chātā linuhos
He might arrive late if the mountain road is blocked.
पहाडको बाटो बन्द भए ढिलो आउन सक्छन्।
pahāḍako bāṭo banda bhae ḍhilo āuna sakchan
We may cancel the picnic if the weather forecast is bad.
मौसमको पूर्वानुमान नराम्रो भए पिकनिक रद्द हुन सक्छ।
mausamako pūrvānumāna narāmro bhae pikanika radda huna sakcha
She might not know the answer; this topic is new for her.
उनलाई जवाफ नथाहा हुन सक्छ; यो विषय नयाँ छ।
unalāī javāpha nathāhā huna sakcha; yo viṣaya nayā~ cha
The bus might be full on Friday evening; leave earlier.
शुक्रबार साँझ बस भरिन सक्छ; छिटो निस्कनुहोस्।
śukrabāra sā~jha basa bharina sakcha; chiṭo niskanuhos
I might join you later if my meeting ends on time.
बैठक समयमै सकियो भने पछि सहभागी हुन सक्छु।
baiṭhaka samayamai sakiyo bhane pachi sahabhāgī huna sak-chu
They might sell the old house next year; nothing is certain.
अर्को वर्ष पुरानो घर बेच्न सक्छन्; निश्चित छैन।
arko varṣa purāno ghara becna sakchan; niścita chaina
You might feel dizzy after this medicine; drink water.
यो औषधि खाएपछि चक्कर लाग्न सक्छ; पानी पिउनुहोस्।
yo auṣadhi khāepachi cakkara lāgna sakcha; pānī piunuhos
The lights might go off during the storm; keep a torch ready.
आँधीमा बत्ती जान सक्छ; बत्ती तयार राख्नुहोस्।
ā~dhīmā battī jāna sakcha; battī tayāra rākhnuhos
He might have forgotten; remind him gently tomorrow.
We might walk if we cannot find a shared taxi.
It might snow on the pass though the valley is clear.
उपत्यका खुला भए पनि घाटीमा हिउँ पर्न सक्छ।
upatyakā khulā bhae pani ghāṭīmā hiu~ parna sakcha
She might call tonight; keep your phone charged.
आज रात फोन गर्न सक्छिन्; मोबाइल चार्ज राख्नुहोस्।
āja rāta phona garna sakkchin; mobāila cārja rākhnuhos
The price might go up after the festival; buy rice now.
चाडपछि मूल्य बढ्न सक्छ; अहिले चामल किन्नुहोस्।
cāḍapachi mūlya baḍhna sakcha; ahile cāmala kinnuhos
I might be wrong about the time; please check the ticket.
समयमा म गलत हुन सक्छु; टिकट हेर्नुहोस्।
samayamā ma galata huna sak-chu; ṭikaṭa hernuhos

title: Adjectives made from verb in Nepali
content: Verbal adjectives: -एको/-एकी participles (गरेको, पढेको). Example: पढेको केटा (boy who has studied / who reads).
intro: Present: verb + -ैत. Past: verb + -ल. Reading boy = पढ़ैत नेना.

blocks.heading: Adjectives from verbs
rows: The boiled potatoes are still hot; wait before you peel them.
उमालेको आलु अझै तातो छ; नछोड्नुअघि पर्खनुहोस्।
umāleko ālu ajhai tāto cha; nachoḍnuaghi parkhanuhos
I love fried fish from the river when it is fresh.
ताजा भए नदीको तारेको माछा मन पर्छ।
tāja bhae nadīko tāreko māchā mana parcha
The written exam is easier than the oral test for me.
मुख परीक्षाभन्दा लिखित परीक्षा सजिलो छ।
mukha parīkṣābhandā likhita parīkṣā sajilo cha
We need a printed copy; the handwritten one is hard to read.
छापिएको प्रतिलिपि चाहिन्छ; हस्तलेखन पढ्न गाह्रो।
chāpieko pratilipi cāhincha; hastalekhana paḍhna gāhro
The painted wall looks bright after the monsoon wash.
मनसुनपछि रङ्ग लगाएको भित्ता उज्यालो देखिन्छ।
manasunapachi raṅga lagāeko bhittā ujyālo dekhincha
Tired travellers sat on the broken bench near the tap.
थाकेका यात्री टुटेको बेन्चमा ट्याप नजिक बसे।
thākekā yātrī ṭuṭeko bemcamā ṭyāpa najika base
The locked gate stopped strangers from entering the yard.
बन्द गेटले अजनबीलाई आँगन पस्न दिएन।
banda geṭale ajanabīlāī āṅgana pasna diena
She wore a knitted scarf though the morning was mild.
बिहान न्यानो भए पनि बुनेको मफलर लगाइन्।
bihāna nyāno bhae pani buneko maphalar lagāin
The spilled oil made the kitchen floor dangerously slippery.
I prefer steamed momos to fried ones on hot days.
गर्मीमा तलेकोभन्दा भापेको मोमो मन पर्छ।
garimāmā talekobhandā bhāpeko momo mana parcha
The closed shop had a notice about the family emergency.
बन्द पसलमा परिवारको आपतको सूचना थियो।
banda pasalamā parivārako āpatako sūcanā thiyo
Chopped onions made everyone cry in the small kitchen.
सानो भान्सामा काटेको प्याजले सबैलाई रुवायो।
sāno bhānsāmā kāṭeko pyājale sabailāī ruvāyo
The mixed pickle tastes better after two weeks in the sun.
दुई हप्ता घाममा राखेपछि अचार बढी मिठो।
duī haptā ghāmamā rākhepachi acāra baḍhī miṭho
Baked bread from the village oven has a special smell.
गाउँको भट्टीबाट पाकेको रोटीको गन्ध फरक छ।
gāu~ko bhaṭṭībāṭa pākeko roṭīko gandha pharka cha
The injured bird could not fly until we cared for it.
घाइते चरा हेरचाह नगर्दासम्म उड्न सकेन।
ghāite carā heracāha nagardāsamma uḍna sakena

title: Adjectives related to prepositions in Nepali
content: Adjectives from place/relation: घरको (of the house), भित्रको (inside), बाहिरको (outside).
intro: Examples: भित्रको कोठा (inner room), बाहिरको बाटो (outer road), मसँग (with me).

blocks.heading: Adjective + preposition
rows: The upstairs flat is quieter than the one facing the main road.
माथिको फ्ल्याट मुख्य सडकतर्फकोभन्दा शान्त छ।
māthiko phlyāṭ mukhya saḍakatarphakobhandā śānta cha
Put the keys inside the drawer before you leave.
निस्कनु अघि चाबी दराज भित्र राख्नुहोस्।
niskanu aghi cābī darāja bhitra rākhnuhos
We sat outside the temple until the rain stopped.
The cat hid under the bed when the fireworks started.
He stood behind the door and listened to the conversation.
The fruit seller sat beside the road under a plastic sheet.
प्लास्टिक तिरपालमुनि फलफूल बेच्ने बाटोछेउमा बसे।
plāsṭika tirapālamuni phalphūla becne bāṭochema base
We walked along the canal until we reached the bridge.
The notice on the wall above the switch says do not touch.
स्विचमाथिको भित्ताको सूचनामा नछुनु भनिएको छ।
svicamāthikō bhittāko sūcanāmā nacunu bhanieko cha
She lives across the street from the old post office.
purāno hulāka ko pāripaṭṭi unī bas-chin
The keys fell between the car seats and we could not find them.
चाबी गाडीको सिट बीचमा खस्यो, भेटिएन।
cābī gāḍīko siṭa bīcama khasyo, bheṭiena
He ran toward the station when he heard the whistle.
The dog slept near the fire on the cold winter night.
जाडो रात आगो नजिक कुकुर सुत्यो।
jāḍo rāta āgo najika kukura sutyo
Keep the medicine out of reach of small children.
औषधि साना बच्चाको हात नपुग्ने ठाउँमा राख्नुहोस्।
auṣadhi sānā baccāko hāta napugne ṭhāu~mā rākhnuhos
We met at the cafe opposite the college gate.
The bird flew over the roof and disappeared behind the hill.

title: Using May to express wish in Nepali
content: Wish: होय (may it be). Long live = जियत रहू. May you be happy = अहाँक खुशी होय.
intro: Blessing: अहाँक मंगल होए. May God bless = भगवान आशीर्वाद देओ.

blocks.heading: May (wish)
rows: May your new year be full of peace and good health.
नयाँ वर्ष शान्ति र स्वास्थ्यले भरियोस्।
nayā~ varṣa śānti ra svāsthryale bhariyos
May the exam go well for every student in our class.
हाम्रो कक्षाका सबै विद्यार्थीलाई परीक्षा राम्रो होस्।
hāmro kakṣākā sabai vidyārthīlāī parīkṣā rāmro hos
May the rains come on time so the farmers can smile.
May you recover quickly and join us at work again.
छिटो निको होस्, फेरि काममा सँगै होऔं।
chiṭo niko hos, pheri kāmamā saṃgai hō auṃ
May your journey be safe and your family stay calm at home.
यात्रा सुरक्षित होस्, घर परिवार शान्त रहोस्।
yātrā surakṣita hos, ghara parivāra śānta rahos
May this small gift remind you of our friendship.
May the children grow up honest and kind in this village.
यस गाउँमा बच्चा इमानदार र दयालु हुन्उन्।
yasa gāu~mā baccā imāndāra ra dayālu hunun
May your parents live long enough to see your success.
आमाबुबाले तपाईंको सफलता देखून्, दीर्घायु होस्।
āmābubāle tapāīṃko saphalata dekhūn, dīrghāyu hos
May the lights never go out in your home during festivals.
चाडमा घर उज्यालो रहोस्, बत्ती नजाओस्।
cāḍamā ghara ujyālo rahos, battī najaos
May we meet again under better circumstances next time.
May your voice stay strong when you speak for the poor.
गरिबका लागि बोल्दा स्वर बलियो रहोस्।
garibakā lāgi boldā svara baliyo rahos
May this marriage bring patience and respect to both families.
यो विवाहले दुवै परिवारमा धैर्य र सम्मान होस्।
yo vivāhale duvai parivāramā dhairya ra sammāna hos
May the road be smooth when you drive home tonight.
आज रात घर फर्कँदा बाटो सजिलो होस्।
āja rāta ghara pharka~dā bāṭo sajilo hos
May your worries decrease a little after this good news.
यो राम्रो खबरपछि चिन्ता अलि घटोस्।
yo rāmro khabarapachi cintā ali ghaṭos
May our country stay peaceful and united in hard times.
कठिन बेलामा देश शान्त र एक रहोस्।
kaṭhina belāmā deśa śānta ra eka rahos

title: Phrase "To Make Someone do something" in Nepali
content: Causative: लगाउनु, गराउनु. I made him go = मैले उसलाई जान लगाएँ.
intro: Make eat = खुवाब. Make read = पढ़ाब. Get something done = कराब.

blocks.heading: Make someone do
rows: The teacher made the students rewrite the essay neatly.
शिक्षकले विद्यार्थीलाई निबन्ध फेरि सफा लेख्न लगाए।
śikṣakale vidyārthīlāī nibandha pheri saphā lekhna lagāe
His mother made him apologise to the neighbour's child.
आमाले छिमेकीको बच्चासँग माफी माग्न लगाइन्।
āmāle chimekīko baccāsa~ga māphī māgna lagāin
The rain made the road slippery for morning traffic.
पानीले बाटो चिप्लो बनायो, बिहान भीड भयो।
pānīle bāṭo ciplo banāyo, bihāna bhīḍa bhayo
I will not make you wait outside in this cold wind.
She made everyone laugh with a story about her village.
The loud noise made the baby cry in the crowded hall.
कोलाहलले भीड भएको हलमा बच्चा रुवायो।
kolahalale bhīḍa bhaeko halamā baccā ruvāyo
We made him drink warm water when he felt dizzy.
चक्कर लाग्दा तातो पानी पिउन लगायौं।
cakkara lāgdā tāto pānī piuna lagāyauṃ
Do not make the dog run on the hot road in midday.
दिउँसो गर्मी सडकमा कुकुर दौडाउनु हुँदैन।
diuṃso garimī saḍakamā kukura dauḍāunu hu~dain
The film made us think about our own childhood.
She made the clerk check the account twice before paying.
तिर्नुअघि लेखापाललाई खाता दुई पटक जाँच गर्न लगाइन्।
tirnuaghi lekhāpālalāī khātā duī paṭaka jā̃ca garna lagāin
The smell of joss sticks made the room feel calm.
He made his son carry the heavy bag to teach responsibility.
जिम्मेवारी सिकाउन छोरालाई भारी झोला बोक्न लगाए।
jimmevārī sikāuna chorālāī bhārī jholā bokna lagāe
The news made her silent for the rest of the evening.
I will make you taste this pickle if you promise to be honest.
सत्य बोल्छौ भने यो अचार चाख्न लगाउँछु।
satya bolchau bhane yo acāra cākhna lagāu~chu
The coach made the team run an extra lap after the mistake.
गल्तीपछि प्रशिक्षकले टिमलाई थप चक्का दौडाए।
galtīpachi praśikṣakale ṭimalāī thapa cakkā dauḍāe

title: Generalizing words – somewhere, somehow, anything, anybody etc. in Nepali
content: General: कतबो (somewhere), केओ (someone), किछु (something), कोनो (any).
intro: Somewhere = कतबो. Anyone = कोनो. Something = किछु. Everywhere = हर जगह.

blocks.heading: Generalizing words
rows: Someone left the gate open; we do not know who it was.
कसैले गेट खुला छोड्यो; को थाहा छैन।
kasaile geṭa khulā choḍyo; ko thāhā chaina
I dropped my keys somewhere in this room but I cannot see them.
यो कोठामा कतै चाबी खस्यो, देखिँदैन।
yo koṭhāmā katai cābī khasyo, dekhidaīna
Somehow we reached the station though we missed the first bus.
कुनै न कुनै तरिकाले पहिलो बस छुटे पनि स्टेशन पुग्यौं।
kunai na kunai tarikāle pahilo basa chuṭe pani sṭeśana pugyauṃ
Anything you cook tonight is fine; I am not fussy.
आज रात जे पनि पकाउनुहुन्छ हुन्छ, म रोज्दैन।
āja rāta je pani pakāunuhuncha huncha, ma rojdaina
Nobody answered the door though we knocked several times.
धेरै पटक ढकढक्याए पनि कसैले ढोका खोलेन।
dherai paṭaka ḍhakaḍhakyāe pani kasaile ḍhokā kholena
I will eat somewhere on the way if I feel hungry.
भोक लाग्यो भने बाटोमै कतै खान्छु।
bhoka lāgyo bhane bāṭomai katai khān-chu
Something is burning in the kitchen; go and check quickly.
भान्सामा केही पोलिरहेको छ, चाँडो हेर्नुस्।
bhānsāmā kehī poliraheko cha, cā~ḍo hernus
Everyone was looking for somebody who could speak English.
Nothing in this box is valuable; you can throw it away.
यो डब्बामा केही मूल्यवान छैन, फ्याँक्न सकिन्छ।
yo ḍabbāmā kehī mūlyavāna chaina, phyā~kna sakinchha
Any bus that goes to Ratna Park will work for us.
रत्न पार्क जाने जुनसुकै बस हुन्छ।
ratna pārka jāne junasukai basa huncha
Somewhere in this pile of papers lies the signed form.
कागजको ढेरमै कतै दस्तखत भएको फारम छ।
kāgajako ḍheramai katai dastakhata bhaeko phārama cha
Somebody has been using my towel; I can smell a different soap.
मेरो तौलिया कोहीले प्रयोग गर्छ, अर्को साबुनको गन्ध।
mero tauliyā kohīle prayog gar-cha, arko sābunako gandha
Nothing much happened today except the usual office work.
आज साधारण कार्यालयको काम बाहेक केही भएन।
āja sādhāraṇa kāryālayako kāma bāheka kehi bhaena
Anyone who knows the answer may raise a hand quietly.
जवाफ थाहा भए कोहीले चुपचाप हात उठाउन सक्छ।
javāpha thāhā bhae kohīle cupcāpa hāta uṭhāuna sakcha
I left my umbrella somewhere near the tea stall yesterday.
हिजो चियापसल नजिकै कतै छाता छोडें।
hijo ciyāpasala najikai katai chātā choḍe~

title: "To have" to indicate possession and relation in Nepali
content: Have/possession: छ, छैन with मेरो / मसँग (e.g. मसँग पैसा छ — I have money).
intro: I have a book = मेरो किताब छ. He has a house = उसको घर छ.

blocks.heading: To have (possession)
rows: I have two brothers and one sister in my family.
मेरो परिवारमा दुई भाइ र एक बहिनी छन्।
mero parivāramā duī bhāi ra eka bahinī chan
She has a small shop near the bridge that sells snacks.
पुल नजिक उनको सानो पसल छ, नास्ता पाइन्छ।
pula najika unako sāno pasala cha, nāstā pāincha
We have no car but we manage with a bicycle and the bus.
गाडी छैन, साइकल र बसले काम चल्छ।
gāḍī chaina, sāikala ra basale kāma cal-cha
He has grey hair now though he still jokes like a young man.
अहिले कपाल फुस्रो छ तर जवान जस्तै ठट्टा गर्छ।
ahile kapāla phusro cha tara javāna jastai ṭhaṭṭā gar-cha
My uncle has three sons and they all work in the city.
काकाका तीन छोरा छन्, सबै सहरमा काम गर्छन्।
kākākā tīna chorā chan, sabai saharamā kāma gar-chan
This house has a leaky roof during heavy rain.
धेरै पानी परे यो घरको छाना चुहिन्छ।
dherai pānī pare yo gharako chānā cuhincha
I have a slight fever since last night.
We have relatives in both Kathmandu and Pokhara.
She has patience when children ask the same question again.
बच्चाले फेरि सोध्दा पनि धैर्य छ।
baccāle pheri sodhdā pani dhairya cha
The old tree has a hollow where birds build nests.
पुरानो रुखमा प्वाल छ, चराले गुड राख्छन्।
purāno rukhamā pvāla cha, carāle guḍa rāk-chan
I have nothing more to say about this matter today.
आज यस विषयमा अरू केही भन्न छैन।
āja yasa viṣayamā arū kehi bhanna chaina
He has a good name in the village because he helps the poor.
गरिबलाई सहयोग गर्छ, गाउँमा राम्रो नाम छ।
garibalāī sahayoga gar-cha, gāu~mā rāmro nāma cha
We have been neighbours for ten years and never quarrelled.
दस वर्षदेखि छिमेकी, कहिल्यै झगडा भएन।
dasa varṣadekhi chimekī, kahilyai jhagaḍā bhaena
This phone has a weak battery; charge it every night.
यो फोनको ब्याट्री कमजोर छ, राति चार्ज गर्नुहोस्।
yo phonako byāṭrī kamajora cha, rāti cārja garnuhos
She has courage to speak the truth in front of powerful people.
बलियो मान्छे अगाडि पनि सत्य बोल्न साहस छ।
baliyo mānche agāḍi pani satya bolna sāhasa cha

title: Using "To mean". How to ask meaning in Nepali
content: What does X mean? = X को अर्थ के हो? Meaning = अर्थ, मतलब.
intro: How to ask: यसको अर्थ के हो? (What does this mean?).

blocks.heading: Meaning
rows: What does this Nepali word mean in simple English?
यो नेपाली शब्दको सरल अङ्ग्रेजीमा के अर्थ हो?
yo nepālī śabda ko sarala aṅgrejīmā ke artha ho
I did not mean to hurt you; I spoke without thinking.
This sign means the road is closed for repair.
यो संकेतले बाटो मर्मतका लागि बन्द छ भन्छ।
yo saṅketale bāṭo marmatakā lāgi banda cha bhancha
What do you mean by early — six o'clock or seven?
छिटो भन्नाले छ बजे कि सात बजे?
chiṭo bhannale cha baje ki sāta baje
She meant to call yesterday but her phone had no balance.
हिजो फोन गर्न खोजेकी थिइन् तर ब्यालेन्स थिएन।
hijo phona garna khojekī thiin tara byālensa thiena
The red light means stop even if the street looks empty.
गल्ली खाली भए पनि रातो बत्ती भने रोकिनु पर्छ।
gallī khālī bhae pani rāto battī bhane rokinu parcha
Could you ask the teacher what this sentence means?
शिक्षकलाई यो वाक्यको अर्थ के हो भनेर सोध्नुहोस्।
śikṣakalāī yo vākyako artha ke ho bhanera sodhnuhos
His silence meant he disagreed though he did not argue aloud.
चुप रहनुले असहमति जनायो, जोरले बहस गरेन।
cupa rahanule asamati janāyo, jorle bahasa garena
I do not know what this abbreviation means on the form.
फारममा यो संक्षिप्त रूपको अर्थ थाहा छैन।
phāramā yo saṅkṣipta rūpako artha thāhā chaina
The poem means more than the words say on the surface.
What does it mean when the temple bell rings at noon?
दिउँसो मन्दिरको घन्टी बज्दा के अर्थ हुन्छ?
diuṃso mandirako ghaṇṭī bajdā ke artha huncha
He explained what the new tax rule means for small shops.
नयाँ कर नियमले सानो पसललाई के असर पार्छ भन्ने बुझाए।
nayā~ kara niyamale sāno pasalalāī ke asara pārcha bhanne bujhāe
I meant to bring sweets but the shop was already closed.
मिठाइ ल्याउन खोजेको थिएँ तर पसल बन्द थियो।
miṭhāi lyāuna khojeko thie~ tara pasala banda thiyo
This gesture in dance means welcome in our tradition.
नृत्यमा यो हावभाव हाम्रो परम्परामा स्वागत हो।
nṛtyamā yo hāvabhāva hāmro paramparāmā svāgata ho
Tell me if any word in this list is hard to understand.
यो सूचीमा कुनै शब्द बुझ्न गाह्रो छ भन्नुहोस्।
yo sūcimā kunai śabda bujhna gāhro cha bhannuhos

title: Conjunctions in Nepali – Part 1
content: And = आ, ओ. But = मुदा, किंतु. Because = किएकि. So = तँ. That = जे.
intro: And = आ. But = मुदा. Because = किएकि. So = तँ. Although = हुनकर बावत.

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 1
rows: I bought rice and lentils at the market this morning.
बिहान बजारमा चामल र दाल किनें।
bihāna bajāramā cāmala ra dāla kine~
She wanted tea but the shop had only cold drinks.
चिया चाहिएको थियो तर पसलमा चिसो मात्र थियो।
ciyā cāhieko thiyo tara pasalamā ciso mātra thiyo
We stayed home because the storm was very strong.
आँधी धेरै तेज भएकाले घरमै बस्यौं।
ā~dhī dherai teja bhaekāle gharamai basyauṃ
He studied hard so he could pass the difficult exam.
कडा परीक्षा पास गर्न मेहनत गर्यो।
kaḍā parīkṣā pāsa garna mēhanata garyo
I believe you because you have never lied to me before.
अघि कहिल्यै झुटो नभनेकाले तपाईंलाई विश्वास गर्छु।
aghi kahilyai jhuṭo nabhanekāle tapāīṃlāī viśvāsa gar-chu
She was tired although she slept for eight hours.
आठ घण्टा सुत्दा पनि थाकेकी थिइन्।
āṭha ghaṇṭā sutdā pani thākekī thiin
The road was muddy though the sun came out later.
पछि घाम लागे पनि बाटो हिलो थियो।
pachi ghāma lāge pani bāṭo hilo thiyo
It was late yet we decided to walk home together.
ढिलो भए पनि सँगै हिँडेर जाने निर्णय गर्यौं।
ḍhilo bhae pani saṃgai hiḍera jāne nirṇaya garyauṃ
He is still weak after the fever but he can sit up.
ज्वरोपछि कमजोर छ तर बस्न सक्छ।
jvaropachi kamajora cha tara basna sakcha
Tell me that you will come so I can cook enough rice.
आउनुहुन्छ भने भन्नुहोस्, भात पर्याप्त पकाउँछु।
āunuhuncha bhane bhannuhos, bhāt paryāpta pakāu~chu
I will wait here until the rain stops completely.
पानी पूर्ण रूपले नरोकिञ्जेल यहाँ पर्खिन्छु।
pānī pūrṇa rūpale naroki~jela yahā~ parkhin-chu
We left early since the bus was usually full after eight.
आठ पछि बस भरिन्छ भनेर छिटो निस्कियौं।
āṭha pachi basa bharincha bhannera chiṭo niskiyauṃ
Although the price was high, the quality was excellent.
मूल्य बढी भए पनि गुणस्तर उत्कृष्ट थियो।
mūlya baḍhī bhae pani guṇastara utkṛṣṭha thiyo
She smiled but her eyes showed that she was worried.
I ate breakfast and then I went to the office.
He failed because he did not follow the instructions.

title: Conjunctions in Nepali – Part 2
content: As = जकाँ. Or = या. Neither...nor = न ... न. If = जँ. In case = यदि.
intro: As well as = आ. Or = या. If = जँ. Provided = यदि.

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 2
rows: As the sun rose, the mist lifted from the valley.
You can take tea or coffee; both are on the table.
चिया वा कफी लिनुहोस्; दुवै टेबुलमा छ।
ciyā vā kaphī linuhos; duvai ṭebulamā cha
Neither he nor his brother could repair the old radio.
उसले न भाइले पुरानो रेडियो मर्मत गर्न सकेन।
usale na bhāile purāno reḍiyo marmata garna sakena
She speaks neither Hindi nor English, only Nepali.
हिन्दी पनि होइन अङ्ग्रेजी पनि होइन, नेपाली मात्र।
hindī pani hoina aṅgrejī pani hoina, nepālī mātra
If you hurry, you can catch the seven o'clock bus.
छिटो हुनुभयो भने सात बजेको बस समात्न सक्नुहुन्छ।
chiṭo hunubhayo bhane sāta bajeko basa samātnā saknuhuncha
In case it rains, keep a plastic sheet in your bag.
पानी पर्यो भने झोलामा प्लास्टिक राख्नुहोस्।
pānī paryo bhane jholāmā plāsṭika rākhnuhos
You may leave early provided you finish your report today.
आज प्रतिवेदन सकाउनुभयो भने छिटो जान सक्नुहुन्छ।
āja prativedana sakāunubhayo bhane chiṭo jāna saknuhuncha
As I opened the door, the dog ran out happily.
ढोका खोल्दा कुकुर खुसीले बाहिर दौड्यो।
ḍhokā kholdā kukura khusīle bāhira dauḍyo
We can walk or take a taxi; it depends on the rain.
हिँड्न वा ट्याक्सी; पानीमा निर्भर छ।
hiḍna vā ṭyāksī; pānīmā nirbhara cha
Neither the phone nor the internet worked during the storm.
आँधीमा फोन पनि इन्टरनेट पनि चलेन।
ā~dhīmā phona pani inṭaraneta pani calena
If she calls, tell her I will reach by evening.
फोन गरिन् भने साँझ पुग्छु भन्नुहोस्।
phona garin bhane sā~jha pug-chu bhannuhos
Take an umbrella in case the weather changes suddenly.
You may borrow my book provided you return it next week.
अर्को हप्ता फर्काउनुहुन्छ भने किताब लिनुहोस्।
arko haptā pharkāunuhuncha bhane kitāba linuhos
As the bell rang, the students stood up quietly.
Neither my father nor my mother could read English then.
त्यतिबेला बुबा आमाले अङ्ग्रेजी पढ्न सक्नुहुन्थेन।
tyatibelā bubā āmāle aṅgrejī paḍhna saknuhunthena
If there is no sugar, I will drink plain tea.
चिनी छैन भने खाली चिया पिउँछु।
cinī chaina bhane khālī ciyā piu~chu
She works as a nurse in the same hospital as her cousin.
भिनाजु जस्तै उही अस्पतालमा नर्सको काम गर्छिन्।
bhināju jastai uhī aspatālamā narsako kāma gar-chin

title: Conjunctions in Nepali – Part 3
content: After = बाद. Then = तँ. As far as = जतेक. As long as = जतेक समय. As if = जकाँ.
intro: After = बाद. Then = तँ. As if = जकाँ ओ...

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 3
rows: After we ate lunch, we walked to the river bank.
दिउँसो खाना खाएपछि नदी किनार हिँड्यौं।
diuṃso khānā khāepachi nadī kināra hiḍyauṃ
Finish your homework first, then you may watch television.
पहिले गृहकार्य सकाउनुहोस्, अनि टिभी हेर्नुहोस्।
pahile gr̥hakārya sakāunuhos, ani ṭibhī hernuhos
As far as I know, the shop opens at nine every day.
मलाई थाहा छसम्म हरेक दिन नौ बजे पसल खुल्छ।
malāī thāhā chasamma hareka dina nau baje pasala khulcha
You can stay as long as you want; the guest room is free.
जति बस्नुहुन्छ बस्नुहोस्; पाहुना कोठा खाली छ।
jati basnuhuncha basnuhos; pāhunā koṭhā khālī cha
He looked as if he had not slept for two nights.
After the meeting ended, we drank tea in the corridor.
Then she explained the rule again with a calm voice.
अनि शान्त स्वरले नियम फेरि बुझाइन्।
ani śānta svarale niyama pheri bujhāin
As far as the doctor can tell, the fracture will heal well.
डाक्टरलाई थाहा छसम्म हड्डी राम्रोसँग जोर्नेछ।
ḍākṭaralāī thāhā chasamma haḍḍī rāmrosa~ga jornēcha
Stay in the shade as long as the sun is strong at noon.
दिउँसो घाम तेज छ जति छायामा बस्नुहोस्।
diuṃso ghāma teja cha jati chāyāmā basnuhos
The house looked as if nobody had lived there for years.
वर्षौं कोही नबसेको जस्तो घर देखियो।
varṣauṃ kohī nabaseko jasto ghara dekhiyo
After he heard the news, he could not speak for a minute.
खबर सुनेपछि एक मिनेट बोल्न सकेन।
khabara sunepachi eka mineṭa bolna sakena
Wash your hands first, then touch the baby.
पहिले हात धुनुहोस्, अनि बच्चा छुनुहोस्।
pahile hāta dhunuhos, ani baccā chunuhos
As far as we remember, this rule did not exist last year.
सम्झनसम्म गत वर्ष यो नियम थिएन।
sam̃jhanasamma gata varṣa yo niyama thiena
You can borrow the book as long as you do not fold the pages.
पृष्ठ ननोचिकन जति दिन पढ्न पाइन्छ।
pr̥ṣṭha nanochikana jati dina paḍhna pāincha
She smiled as if she already knew the surprise.
After the power returned, the whole street cheered loudly.
बिजुली आएपछि गल्लीले जोरले ताली बजायो।
bijulī āepachi gallīle jorle tālī bajāyo

title: Conjunctions in Nepali – Part 4
content: As soon as = जहिना. Once = एक बेर. Rather than = बजाय. Instead of = बजाय. So that = जाहिर.
intro: As soon as = जहिना. Once = एक बेर. So that = जाहिर.

blocks.heading: Conjunctions 4
rows: As soon as I opened the window, cold air filled the room.
झ्याल खोल्नेबित्तिकै चिसो हावा कोठामा पस्यो।
jhyāla kholnebittikai ciso hāvā koṭhāmā pasayo
Once you sign this paper, you cannot change your mind easily.
यो कागजमा दस्तखत गर्नेबित्तिकै सजिलै फिर्ता हुँदैन।
yo kāgajamā dastakhata garnebittikai sajilai phirtā hu~dain
I would rather walk than wait another hour for the bus.
अर्को घण्टा बस पर्खनुभन्दा हिँड्न रोज्छु।
arko ghaṇṭā basa parkhnubhandā hiḍna roj-chu
Instead of shouting, she explained the problem calmly.
He whispered so that the sleeping child would not wake up.
As soon as the bell rings, leave the hall in a line.
Once you taste this pickle, you will ask for the recipe.
She would rather stay hungry than eat food without offering God.
Instead of buying plastic bags, carry a cloth bag from home.
प्लास्टिक किन्नुभन्दा घरबाट कपडाको झोला ल्याउनुहोस्।
plāsṭika kinnubhandā gharabāṭa kapaḍāko jholā lyāunuhos
Speak clearly so that everyone in the back row can hear you.
पछाडिको पङ्क्तिमा सबैले सुनुन् भनेर स्पष्ट बोल्नुहोस्।
pachāḍiko paṅktimā sabaile sunun bhannera spaṣṭa bolnuhos
As soon as we reached home, it started raining heavily.
Once the monsoon starts, the mountain roads become risky.
मनसुन सुरु भए पहाडको बाटो जोखिम हुन्छ।
manasuna suru bhae pahāḍako bāṭo jokhima huncha
I would rather miss the film than travel in an overcrowded bus.
भीडले भरिएको बसभन्दा चलचित्र नहेर्दा हुन्छ।
bhīḍale bharieko basabhandā calacitra naherdā huncha
Instead of blaming others, admit your own mistake honestly.
अरूलाई दोष दिनुभन्दा आफ्नो गल्ती स्वीकार गर्नुहोस्।
arūlāī doṣa dinubhandā āphno galtī svīkāra garnuhos
Close the windows so that mosquitoes do not enter at night.
राति लामखुट्टे नपसुन् भनेर झ्याल बन्द गर्नुहोस्।
rāti lāmakhuṭṭe napasun bhannera jhyāla banda garnuhos

title: List of Conjunctions in Nepali
content: Common conjunctions: आ, मुदा, किएकि, तँ, जे, जँ, या, बाद, जकाँ, जतेक.
intro: Summary list with examples.

blocks.heading: Conjunctions list
rows: म जान चाहन्छु तर समय छैन।
ma jāna cāhanchu tara samaya chaina
यदि पानी पर्यो भने घरमै बसौं।
yadi pānī paryo bhane gharamai basauṃ

title: Combining sentences in Nepali
content: Joining two sentences: जे (that), आ (and), मुदा (but). Relative clause with जे.
intro: Two clauses: म जान्छु कि तपाईं आउनुभयो (I know that you came).

blocks.heading: Combining sentences
rows: I woke early and I finished my work before breakfast.
म छिटो उठें र नास्ताअघि काम सकाएँ।
ma chiṭo uṭhe~ ra nāstāaghi kāma sakāe~
She was tired, yet she cooked dinner for everyone.
थाकेकी थिइन् तैपनि सबैका लागि खाना पकाइन्।
thākekī thiin taipani sabaikā lāgi khānā pakāin
The road was muddy because it had rained all night.
रातभरि पानी परेकाले बाटो हिलो थियो।
rātābhari pānī parekāle bāṭo hilo thiyo
He opened the shop, then he swept the floor carefully.
पसल खोलेर अनि भुइँ सफा गरे।
pasala kholera ani bhui~ saphā gare
We stayed home, for the storm warning was serious.
आँधीको चेतावनी गम्भीर भएकाले घरमै बस्यौं।
ā~dhīko cetāvanī gambhīra bhaekāle gharamai basyauṃ
Although the bus was late, we reached the station on time.
बस ढिलो भए पनि समयमै स्टेशन पुग्यौं।
bas ḍhilo bhae pani samayamai sṭeśana pugyauṃ
She neither shouted nor cried; she simply walked away.
न कराइन् न रोइन्; शान्त हिँडिन्।
na karāin na roin; śānta hiḍin
I like tea while my brother prefers coffee.
मलाई चिया मन पर्छ भाइलाई कफी।
malāī ciyā mana parcha bhāilāī kaphī
If you hurry, you will catch the train; otherwise you will wait.
छिटो हुनुभयो भने रेल समात्नुहोस्, नभए पर्खनुहोस्।
chiṭo hunubhayo bhane rela samātnuhos, nabhae parkhanuhos
He studied hard so that he could win a scholarship.
The dog barked, but the baby did not wake up.
We can take a taxi, or we can walk if you prefer.
ट्याक्सी लिन सकिन्छ वा हिँड्न पनि मिल्छ।
ṭyāksī lina sakinchha vā hiḍna pani milcha
After the exam ended, the students smiled with relief.
परीक्षा सकेपछि विद्यार्थीले राहत मुस्कान देखाए।
parīkṣā sakepachi vidyārthīle rāhata musakāna dekhāe
Neither the phone nor the internet worked during the blackout.
बिजुली गएको बेला फोन पनि इन्टरनेट पनि चलेन।
bijulī gāeko belā phona pani inṭaraneta pani calena

title: Compound verbs in Nepali
content: Compound verb = main verb + auxiliary (ले, दे, पड़, जा). खा लेल, दे देल.
intro: Conjunct verbs: करि देत (do and give), खा लेत (eat up).

blocks.heading: Compound verbs
rows: She looked up the word and wrote it down in her notebook.
We ate up the sweets before anyone could stop us.
He threw away the old shoes after buying new ones.
I sat down and drank water before I spoke again.
They brought in the heavy boxes with help from neighbours.
छिमेकीको मद्दतले भारी डब्बा भित्र ल्याए।
chimekīko maddatale bhārī ḍabbā bhitra lyāe
The child cried out when she saw the stray dog.
We paid off the debt and felt much lighter that evening.
ऋण तिरेर साँझ हल्का महसुस गर्यौं।
ṛṇa tirera sā~jha halka mahasusa garyauṃ
She cut up the vegetables while the oil was heating.
He read through the contract before he signed his name.
I picked up the phone on the second ring.
We locked up the house and left for the station together.
घर बन्द गरेर सँगै स्टेशन लाग्यौं।
ghara banda garera saṃgai sṭeśana lāgyauṃ
The teacher wrote down every mistake on the blackboard.
शिक्षकले कालो पाटीमा हरेक गल्ती लेखे।
śikṣakale kālo pāṭīmā hareka galtī lekhe
He washed off the mud from his bicycle in the yard.
She gave away her old books to the village library.
I threw away the rotten fruit so nobody would eat it.
बिग्रिएको फल फ्याँकें कसैले नखाऊन् भनेर।
bigrieko phala phyā~ke~ kasaile nakhāun bhannera

title: Active Passive voice in Nepali
content: Passive: object as subject, verb in passive form. क' लेल (done), देखल (was seen).
intro: Active: ओ पोथी पढ़ल (He read the book). Passive: पोथी पढ़ल गेल (The book was read).

blocks.heading: Active / Passive
rows: Rice is grown in many Terai fields every year.
हरेक वर्ष तराईका खेतमा धान लगाइन्छ।
hareka varṣa tarāīkā khetamā dhāna lagāincha
The letter was written by her yesterday evening.
पत्र हिजो साँझ उनले लेखेकी हुन्।
patra hijo sā~jha unile lekhekī hun
The gate was locked at ten every night.
हरेक रात दस बजे गेट बन्द गरिन्थ्यो।
hareka rāta dasa baje geṭa banda garinthyo
Mistakes were found in the report after careful checking.
The old bridge was repaired before the monsoon.
English is taught in this school from grade one.
यो स्कुलमा कक्षा एकदेखि अङ्ग्रेजी पढाइन्छ।
yo skulamā kakṣā ekadekhi aṅgrejī paḍhāincha
The sweets were eaten before the guests arrived.
पाहुना आउनु अघि नै मिठाइ सकियो।
pāhunā āunu aghi nai miṭhāi sakiyo
A new road is being built near our village.
The lost bag was returned to the owner by the police.
Tea is served with biscuits in our office.
The exam was cancelled because of the strike.
The song was sung by children at the school programme.
Water is boiled before it is poured into the bottle.
The rules were explained again at the morning meeting.
The thief was caught by people near the market.

title: Active Passive voice in Nepali – Style 2
content: Passive with य/जा (go): कराब (to be done), देखाब (to be shown).
intro: Infinitive passive: खाब (to eat) → खाब याब (to be eaten).

blocks.heading: Passive style 2
rows: Let the work be finished today without any excuse.
आज नै काम सकियोस्, बहाना बिना।
āja nai kāma sakiyos, bahānā binā
May this message be read by everyone in the class.
The door had been left open by mistake.
Nothing more should be said about this matter in public.
सार्वजनिक रूपमा यस विषयमा अरू नबोलियोस्।
sārvajanika rūpamā yasa viṣayamā arū naboliyos
The bill must be paid before the fifteenth of this month.
यो महिनाको पन्ध्र गते अघि बिल तिर्नु पर्छ।
yo mahīnāko pandhra gate aghi bila tirnu parcha
All seats were taken when we reached the hall.
हामी हल पुग्दा सबै सिट भरिएको थियो।
hāmī hala pugdā sabai siṭa bharieko thiyo
The news was expected by everyone in the village.
गाउँका सबैले खबरको प्रतीक्षा गरेका थिए।
gāu~kā sabaile khabarako pratīkṣā gareka thie
The old tree might be cut if it becomes dangerous.
खतरनाक भए पुरानो रुख काटिन सक्छ।
khatarānāka bhae purāno rukha kāṭina sakcha
Help should be given to anyone who asks politely.
The form has to be signed in blue ink only.
फारम नीलो स्याहीले मात्र दस्तखत गर्नु पर्छ।
phārama nīlo syāhile mātra dastakhata garnu parcha
The meeting was postponed because of the chief guest's delay.
मुख्य अतिथि ढिलो भएकाले बैठक सरेको थियो।
mukhya atithi ḍhilo bhaekāle baiṭhaka sareko thiyo
Fresh vegetables are sold here every morning.
हरेक बिहान यहाँ ताजा तरकारी बेचिन्छ।
hareka bihāna yahā~ tāja tarkārī becincha
The lost child was found near the temple pond.
हराएको बच्चा मन्दिरको पोखरी नजिक भेटियो।
harāeko baccā mandirako pokharī najika bheṭiyo
Your name will be called when your turn comes.
The road was blocked by fallen rocks during the night.
राति ढुङ्गा खसेर बाटो बन्द भयो।
rāti ḍhuṅgā khasera bāṭo banda bhayo

title: Using "To Feel" & "To think" in Nepali
content: To feel = महसुस गर्नु, लाग्नु. To think = सोच्नु. I think = मलाई लाग्छ / म सोच्छु.
intro: I feel = मलाई लाग्छ. I think = म सोच्छु.

blocks.heading: Feel / Think
rows: I feel cold in this room because the window is open.
झ्याल खुला भएकाले कोठामा चिसो लाग्छ।
jhyāla khulā bhaekāle koṭhāmā ciso lāgcha
She feels nervous before every exam though she studies well.
राम्रो पढ्दा पनि हरेक परीक्षाअघि घबराउँछिन्।
rāmro paḍhdā pani hareka parīkṣāaghi ghabarāu~chin
We feel grateful for your help during the flood.
He feels pain in his shoulder when he lifts heavy bags.
I think this plan will work if we start early.
छिटो सुरु गरे यो योजना चल्छ जस्तो लाग्छ।
chiṭo suru gare yo yojanā cal-cha jasto lāgcha
She thinks honesty is more important than quick money.
छिटो पैसाभन्दा इमानदारी महत्त्वपूर्ण छ भन्छिन्।
chiṭo paisābhandā imāndārī mahattvapūrṇa cha bhanchin
They think the bus strike will end tomorrow.
भोलि बस हडताल सकिन्छ जस्तो उनीहरूलाई लाग्छ।
bholi basa haḍatāla sakinchha jasto unīharūlāī lāgcha
Do you feel better after taking the medicine?
I thought you were angry but you were only tired.
रिसाएको जस्तो लागेको थियो, थाकेको रहेछ।
risāeko jasto lāgeko thiyo, thākeko rahecha
She feels lonely in the city though she has many neighbours.
धेरै छिमेकी भए पनि सहरमा एक्लो लाग्छ।
dherai chimekī bhae pani saharamā eklo lāgcha
We think it might rain because the wind turned cold.
हावा चिसो भएकाले पानी पर्ला जस्तो लाग्छ।
hāvā ciso bhaekāle pānī paralā jasto lāgcha
He thinks too much about small problems at night.
I feel proud when my students succeed.
What do you think about moving to a quieter neighbourhood?
शान्त ठाउँ सर्ने बारे के लाग्छ?
śānta ṭhāu~ sarne bāre ke lāgcha
She felt dizzy and sat down on the nearest bench.

title: Idioms and Phrases in Nepali
content: Common idioms and set phrases in Nepali.
intro: Time is money = समय धन हो. Where there is a will there is a way = इच्छा भए मार्ग खुल्छ.

blocks.heading: Idioms and phrases
rows: Do not count your chickens before they hatch.
Where there is smoke, there is fire.
धुवाँ छ भने आगो पनि हुन्छ।
dhuvā~ cha bhane āgo pani huncha
A friend in need is a friend indeed.
संकटमा साथ दिने साथी साँचो साथी।
saṅkaṭamā sātha dine sāthī sā~co sāthī
The early bird catches the worm.
Too many cooks spoil the broth.
धेरै मान्छेले एउटै काम गर्दा बिग्रिन्छ।
dherai mānchele euṭai kāma gardā bigrincha
A stitch in time saves nine.
समयमै सिलाइ गर्दा ठूलो खर्च बच्छ।
samayamai silāi gardā ṭhūlo kharca bac-cha
Every cloud has a silver lining.
Rome was not built in a day.
The pen is mightier than the sword.
When in Rome, do as the Romans do.

title: Idioms and Phrases in Nepali – Part 2
content: More idioms: actions speak louder, easy come easy go, etc.
intro: Actions speak louder than words = शब्दहरू भन्दा कार्यहरू ठूलो स्वरले बोल्छन्।

blocks.heading: Idioms part 2
rows: Kill two birds with one stone.
It is no use crying over spilt milk.
The ball is in your court.
Break the ice at the party.
Hit the nail on the head.
रात अबेरसम्म पढ्नु वा काम गर्नु।
rāta aberasamma paḍhnu vā kāma garnu
Cost an arm and a leg.
Let the cat out of the bag.

title: Sentences using "If-Then" Style 2 in Nepali
content: Conditional type 2: unreal or hypothetical. जँ ... होत तँ ... होत.
intro: If I had known = मैले थाहा पाएको भए. If you had come = तिमी आएको भए.

blocks.heading: If-Then style 2
rows: If you tell me the truth, I will trust you again.
सत्य भन्नुभयो भने फेरि विश्वास गर्छु।
satya bhannubhayo bhane pheri viśvāsa gar-chu
Had you come earlier, we could have eaten together.
Unless you wear a helmet, you cannot ride this bike.
हेलमेट नलगाई यो बाइक चलाउन मिल्दैन।
helameṭa nalagāī yo bāika calāuna mildaina
Suppose it rains — we will cancel the picnic without regret.
पानी पर्यो भने पिकनिक रद्द गर्छौं।
pānī paryo bhane pikanika radda gar-chauṃ
If I were rich, I would build a library in my village.
Even if he apologises, I will not forget the insult.
Whether you agree or not, the meeting will start at ten.
मान्नुहोस् वा नमान्नुहोस्, दस बजे बैठक सुरु हुन्छ।
mānnuhos vā namānnuhos, dasa baje baiṭhaka suru huncha
Provided you finish homework, you may watch one cartoon.
गृहकार्य सकाउनुभयो भने एउटा कार्टुन हेर्न पाइन्छ।
gr̥hakārya sakāunubhayo bhane euṭā kāṭṭuna herna pāincha
If it had not rained, the match would have continued.
Should you need money, call me before Friday.
पैसा चाहियो भने शुक्रबारअघि फोन गर्नुहोस्।
paisā cāhiyo bhane śukrabārāghi phona garnuhos
In case the power goes off, keep candles ready.
बिजुली गयो भने मैनबत्ती तयार राख्नुहोस्।
bijulī gayo bhane mainabattī tayāra rākhnuhos
Were I in your place, I would speak to the teacher first.
म तपाईंको ठाउँमा भए पहिले शिक्षकसँग कुरा गर्थें।
ma tapāīṃko ṭhāu~mā bhae pahile śikṣakasa~ga kurā gar-theṃ
If only you had told me earlier, I could have helped.
अघि नै भन्नुभएको भए मद्दत गर्थें।
aghi nai bhannubhaeko bhae maddata gar-theṃ
Unless everyone agrees, we cannot change the date.
सबै सहमत नभए मिति बदल्न मिल्दैन।
sabai sahamata nabhae miti badalna mildaina
Had she known the truth, she would not have left like that.
सत्य थाहा भए उ त्यसरी गइनन्।
satya thāhā bhae u tyasarī gainan

title: List of Body parts in Nepali
content: Body part names in Nepali.
intro: Head = माथा. Eye = आँख. Hand = हाथ.

content: Common everyday words.
intro: Eat = खाउ. New = नया. Help = सहायता.

title: Relations in Nepali
content: Family and relationship terms.
intro: Father = बाबू. Mother = मां. Brother = भाई.

title: Numbers in Nepali – Part 1
content: Numbers 1 to 20 and basic counting.
intro: One = एक. Two = दू. Three = तीन.

title: Numbers in Nepali – Part 2
content: Numbers 21 to 100 and beyond.
intro: Twenty-one = एक्कीस. Hundred = सय.

title: Fractional numbers, sequence, percentage in Nepali
content: Fractions, order, and percentage.
intro: Half = आधा. Quarter = पाव. Percent = प्रतिशत.

title: Frequently used verbs in Nepali – Part 1
content: Common verbs: to be, to do, to go, to come.
intro: To go = जाब. To come = आब. To do = करब.

title: Frequently used verbs in Nepali – Part 2
content: More common verbs: give, take, want, like.
intro: To give = देब. To take = ल'ब. To want = चाहब.

title: Frequently used verbs in Nepali – Part 3
content: Verbs: work, play, cook, wash, build.
intro: To work = काज करब. To play = खेलब.

title: Frequently used verbs in Nepali – Part 4
content: More verbs: put, keep, change, grow.
intro: To put = राखब. To keep = रखब.

title: One verb, multiple meanings – लावब/देब in Nepali
content: देब (to give) and लावब (to apply/bring) with multiple uses.
intro: Give = देब. Apply = लावब. Put on = लावब.

title: One verb, multiple meanings – लागब in Nepali
content: लागब: to feel, to need, to stick, to start.
intro: To feel = लागब. To need = लागब. To stick = लागब.

title: List of Nepali Adjectives – Part 1
content: Common adjectives: big, small, good, bad.
intro: Big = नमहर. Small = छोट. Good = नीक.

title: Adverbs in Nepali – Part 1
content: Common adverbs: here, there, now, today.
intro: Here = एतय. There = ओतय. Now = अखन.

title: List of adverbs in Nepali
content: More adverbs: inside, outside, up, down, quickly.
intro: Inside = भीतर. Outside = बाहिर. Quickly = तेजी सँ.

title: List of vegetable names in Nepali
content: Common vegetables in Nepali.
intro: Potato = आलू. Tomato = टमाटर. Onion = प्याज.

title: List of names of fruits in Nepali
content: Fruit names in Nepali.
intro: Mango = आम. Banana = केला. Apple = सेब.

title: List of names of birds in Nepali
content: Bird names in Nepali.
intro: Crow = कौआ. Sparrow = गौरैया. Peacock = मोर.

title: List of names of insects in Nepali
content: Insect names in Nepali.
intro: Ant = किरिल. Bee = मधुमक्खी. Mosquito = मच्छर.

title: List of colours in Nepali
content: Colour names in Nepali.
intro: Red = लाल. Blue = नीला. Green = हरियर.

title: List of names of flowers in Nepali
content: Flower names in Nepali.
intro: Rose = गुलाब. Lotus = कमल. Jasmine = चमेली.

title: List of names of animals in Nepali
content: Animal names in Nepali.
intro: Dog = कुकुर. Cat = बिराली. Cow = गाई.

title: Frequently used or common words in Nepali – Miscellaneous
content: Extra common words for daily use.
intro: Yes = हाँ. No = नहि. Please = कृपया.

title: Diwali Festival in Nepali
content: Diwali greetings and festival phrases.
intro: Happy Diwali = दीपावली को शुभकामना.

blocks.heading: Diwali
rows: Diwali is a festival of lights

title: Simple Sentences in Nepali – Introduction/Salutation
content: Greetings and introductions.
intro: Hello = नमस्ते / नमस्कार. My name is... = मेरो नाम ... हो.

blocks.heading: Introduction/Salutation
rows: How do you say ... in Nepali?

title: Simple Nepali Sentences – Time Related
content: Telling time and time-related phrases.
intro: What time is it? = अहिले कति बज्यो?

title: Simple Nepali Sentences – Where/Place Related
content: Asking and telling about places.
intro: Where is...? = ... कहाँ छ?

title: Simple Nepali Sentences – In Hotel
content: Hotel check-in, room, and dining.
intro: I need a room = मलाई कोठा चाहिन्छ.

blocks.heading: In Hotel
rows: Do you have a single room?
I will stay for two days
I want to extend my stay
म मेरो बसाई विस्तार गर्न चाहन्छु
ma mero basāī vistāra garna cāhanchu
Do you have any rooms available?
के तपाईसँग कुनै कोठा उपलब्ध छ?
ke tapāīsa~ga kunai koṭhā upalabdha cha?
Does the room have a bathroom?
May I see the room first?
के म पहिले कोठा हेर्न सक्छु?
ke ma pahile koṭhā herna sakchu?
के तपाईं मलाई _____ मा जगाउन सक्नुहुन्छ?
ke tapāīṃ malāī _____ mā jagāuna saknuhuncha?

title: Simple Nepali sentences – Weather related
content: Weather and season phrases.
intro: How is the weather? = मौसम कस्तो छ?

title: Nepali conversation – with traffic police
content: Stopped by traffic police – phrases.
intro: Where did I go wrong? = म कहाँ गलत भएँ?

blocks.heading: Traffic police
rows: I did not see the signal

title: Simple Nepali conversation – Rickshaw/Taxi driver
content: Hiring a rickshaw or taxi.
intro: Take me to... = मलाई ... लगिदिनुहोस्.

blocks.heading: Rickshaw/Taxi
rows: के तपाईलाई यो ठेगाना थाहा छ?
ke tapāīlāī yo ṭhegānā thāhā cha?

title: Simple Nepali Conversation – Software Engineers
content: Office and tech talk.

blocks.heading: Software Engineers
rows: We have a meeting at ten

title: Simple Nepali conversation – Grocery shop
content: Buying groceries.
intro: Give me one kg = मलाई एक किलो दिनुहोस्.

title: Simple Nepali Conversation – Doctor-Patient
content: At the doctor's.
intro: I have fever = मलाई ज्वरो आएको छ.

title: Simple Nepali Conversation – Teacher & Students
content: Classroom phrases.
intro: Open the book = पोथी खोलू.

title: Simple Nepali conversation – Informal phone conversation
content: Casual phone talk.
intro: Hello, who is this? = हलो, कोन छी?

title: Simple Nepali conversation – Vegetable Market
content: Buying vegetables.
intro: How much for this? = यसको कति पर्छ?

title: Simple Nepali Conversation – at bus stop and in bus
content: Bus travel.
intro: Where does this bus go? = यो बस कहाँ जान्छ?

title: Asking address in Nepali
content: Asking for directions and address.
intro: Where is...? = ... कहाँ छ?

blocks.heading: Asking address
rows: Can you tell me the way?

title: I Love you in Nepali – Proposing someone
content: Expressing love and proposal.
intro: I love you = म तिमीलाई माया गर्छु.

blocks.heading: Love / Proposing
rows: I want to be with you

title: Simple Nepali Conversation – Interview
content: Job interview phrases.
intro: Tell me about yourself = अपन बारे मे बताऊ.

blocks.heading: Interview
rows: Why do you want this job?
I work well in a team
I can join from next month
म अर्को महिनादेखि सामेल हुन सक्छु
ma arko mahinādekhi sāmela huna sakchu

title: Simple Nepali conversation – with housemaid
content: Talking to domestic help.
intro: Clean the room = कमरा साफ करू.

rows: I will pay you on the first

title: Simple Nepali conversation: Linking AADHAR card to mobile number
content: Aadhar-mobile linking at a shop or bank.
intro: I want to link Aadhar to mobile = म मोबाइलमा आधार लिंक गर्न चाहन्छु।

blocks.heading: Aadhar to mobile
rows: I want to link Aadhar to mobile
